At fmRatio=0.6 → modulator ratio 2.8 (a deliberately NON-integer c:m ≈ 1:2.8), which seeds inharmonic FM sidebands at |c±k·m| = a clangy, metallic excitation rather than a clean click. Carrier base freq is velocity-driven 400→2500 Hz with a fast amp-env (80 ms) and faster mod-index env (30 ms, index 0.5→3.0 rad with velocity) — this hot, fast-decaying inharmonic burst is what strikes the Membrane and gives the kick its aggressive synthetic "FM clang" attack, distinct from the raised-cosine click of an 808/909 kick or the self-oscillating squeal of the Buchla/Feedback kick.

params.rationale: The archetype IS the FM exciter — Chowning bell-FM burst strikes the body with an inharmonic, velocity-bright transient.
Kick needs a low tunable fundamental + glide; Membrane gives slightly inharmonic low modes and is the ONLY body that supports tensionMod glide.
Low material = darker, woody-leaning body so the sub stays weighty; the FM exciter + secondary shell supply the metallic top, not the membrane.
Large/low membrane so the un-glided body fundamental sits in kick territory (~70 Hz); the pitch env then sweeps the heard f0 300→30 Hz.
Punchy synthetic kick: enough sub weight, but percussive — not a drone. 808 center ~300 ms; this is a touch tighter for an aggressive kick.
Near-center strike weights the low axisymmetric (0,n) modes → strong fundamental, fewer high diametric modes; classic centered-beater kick.
Kick is a loud lead drum; high but below 1.0 so the FM transient + sub have headroom under the −1 dBTP bus limiter.
If/when the tone-shaper filter engages, LP is the right shape for a kick (tame FM hash above the body).
Gently roll off the most abrasive FM sideband fizz above ~4 kHz while keeping click definition; engages the SVF (cutoff<20k).
Slight emphasis at the LP corner for a touch of focus; not a screaming resonance.
Pitch-glide handles the dynamic spectral move; a filter sweep would double up and muddy it. Left neutral.

params.physicalTarget: internalDrive = 1+0.3·9 = 3.7, recipSqrt waveshaper, unity small-signal makeup
params.rationale: Adds odd-harmonic grit/aggression (flavour, not level after M-2 makeup) to fatten the FM kick.
Wavefolding is a west-coast/Buchla flavour reserved for the feedback kick; FM clang already supplies the aggression. Default-off.
Brief-specified glide start; a pitched 'tonk' onset that reads as a kick attack.
Brief-specified settle; deep sub fundamental (below the 49 Hz 808) for an experimental low kick.
Audible kerthump glide (vs the 808's inaudible 6 ms snap) — matches the aggressive/experimental character. Also the master enable for the pitch env.
Classic 808-style exponential pitch fall: most of the 300→30 Hz drop happens in the first few ms for a punchy 'thud'.
Slight partial-spacing stretch beyond the physical 1.0 nudges the body toward the 'slightly inharmonic synthetic' brief without going full bell.
Mild positive skew shortens the higher inharmonic modes faster than the sub → tight attack clang that resolves to a clean low boom.
A clean 1/k harmonic stack would tonalise/tame the kick; the FM exciter + scatter already supply the spectral complexity. Default-off.
Louder=brighter dynamic harmonic excitation — gives the kick velocity-dependent bite/snap that mimics a struck membrane driven into nonlinearity.
Brief-specified noise layer: a modest air/scuff bed thickening the transient without turning the kick into a snare.
Kick noise is low-mid 'air'/beater, not hiss — keep the noise bed dark so it sits under the sub.
Gentle, broad noise filter — no pitched ring in the noise bed.
Short noise burst tied to the attack/transient, not a sustained tail.
Brown noise concentrates energy low — the right spectral tilt for kick air/thump rather than bright hiss.
Defined leading edge for the FM kick; kept moderate because the FM burst already supplies most of the aggressive transient.
Short, snappy beater contact for a tight attack.
Kick click energy lives ~3–8 kHz; 3.6 kHz gives a sharp tick without ice-pick fizz.

params.physicalTarget: b1 = 0.2 + 0.18·49.8 ≈ 9.2 s^-1 flat damping (RT60 ~ a few hundred ms)
params.rationale: Explicit flat-damping floor so the sub fundamental rings with weight (overrides the Decay-derived floor) but still decays percussively.
Tame the highest membrane modes so the FM clang—not a whistly head—owns the top end; keeps the sub clean.

params.physicalTarget: 15% toward the Rossing air-loaded curve (mostly pure Bessel)
params.rationale: Low air-loading keeps the lowest modes from being pulled into a near-harmonic clean stack — preserves the 'slightly inharmonic synthetic' character the brief wants.

params.physicalTarget: ~4.5% sinusoidal frequency dither on modal freqs
params.rationale: The brief's 'modeScatter' / chaotic character — detunes the modes off pure ratios for an organic, aggressive, slightly unstable timbre.
Drives the metallic secondary body the brief specifies; enough to add an audible inharmonic ring on top of the membrane sub.
Required to realise the metallic secondary body (0.60/0.85) coupling.
Brief-specified 0.60: a smaller, higher metallic shell that rings above the membrane sub for the metallic-coupling timbre.

params.physicalTarget: shell b3 = 0.85·1e-5 (bright, long highs); b1 = max(25,2.5·bodyB1)+0.15·15
params.rationale: Brief-specified 0.85: metallic shell (low high-freq damping → long ringing inharmonic partials) = the 'metallic secondary body'.

params.physicalTarget: amtEff = 0.5·3.3e-5; energy-driven pitch jump up to +3 semitones, relaxing as body decays
params.rationale: Brief-specified mid tension (0.50): adds an energy-dependent upward pitch bend on the attack that relaxes — the 'kerthump' nonlinear membrane glide on top of the pitch env. Membrane-only.
Brief-specified 0.6: a strongly INHARMONIC c:m ratio (>1:3-ish) that makes the FM excitation metallic/clangy rather than a clean bell — the core of the aggressive synthetic character.
Kick is the anchor of the mix — dead center.
Active lead pad.

defaultedParams.why: Filter Env Amount is neutral (no cutoff modulation), so the filter ADSR is inaudible — left at defaults.
Pitch-glide owns the dynamic spectral move; a parallel filter sweep would muddy it. Kept at the no-op center.
Wavefolding is the west-coast/Buchla-kick flavour; the FM clang supplies aggression here, so fold stays bypassed (exact dry).
A clean 1/k injected harmonic series would tonalise/tame the kick; FM + scatter already give spectral richness. Zero-leak bypass.
No need for a per-note material timbre sweep — the pitch env + FM index env already animate the spectrum. Morph off.
A kick is not part of an open/closed mute pair; no choke.
Kick goes to the main bus so the master limiter/glue applies.
No-op for FMImpulse (Feedback-exciter only) — that is the OTHER (Buchla) kick's mechanism.
No-op for FMImpulse (NoiseBurst-exciter only).
No-op for FMImpulse (Friction-exciter only).
Per-pad participation in the GLOBAL cross-pad sympathetic engine; only audible if the kit enables Global Coupling — left neutral, this is a kit-level decision.
Macros are neutral one-knob proxies; all underlying params are voiced explicitly, so every macro stays at 0.5 (zero delta) to preserve those exact values.
A single-segment 300→30 Hz exponential glide is the classic kick shape; the second breakpoint is unnecessary, so knee stays OFF and the mid/curve2 params are inert.

physics:
RESEARCH-DERIVED ACOUSTIC + SYNTHESIS PROFILE.

(A) Kick fundamental & glide (the part that makes it read as a KICK). Acoustic kicks sit ~40–80 Hz fundamental; drum-machine kicks go lower (<50 Hz). The Roland TR-808 bass drum is a bridged-T resonator self-oscillating at ~49–56 Hz (G1), with a fast ~6 ms attack pitch-rise to ~130 Hz and then a slow downward drift through the 50–800 ms decay (center ~300 ms). The "kerthump"/pitch-drop is the perceptual signature of every good kick. → I voice a pitch envelope 300 Hz → 30 Hz (the brief specifies it) with a fast/exponential segment-1 curve so the ear hears a deep "boom-thud" glide; 30 Hz settle puts the sustained body in true sub territory, 300 Hz start gives a pitched "tonk" onset. PitchEnv Time ~75 ms is long enough to be an audible glide (not the inaudible 6 ms 808 snap) — appropriate for the EXPERIMENTAL/aggressive flavour requested. The pitch env rescales ALL membrane modes (post-audit H-3: glide now drives every body), so the inharmonic upper modes glide down with the fundamental.

(B) Membrane mode structure / inharmonicity. A circular drumhead's modes are at Bessel-zero ratios — (0,1)=1.000, (1,1)=1.593, (2,1)=2.136, (0,2)=2.296, (3,1)=2.653, (1,2)=2.918 … i.e. INHARMONIC, unlike a string. This is exactly the source of a real drum's "non-pitched" character. Membrum's MembraneMapper uses these ratios (kMembraneRatios) with f0=500·0.1^size. The brief's "slightly inharmonic synthetic kick" is served by (i) keeping airLoading low (so the lowest modes are NOT pulled down into a clean harmonic-ish stack), and (ii) adding modeScatter (sinusoidal frequency dither ~0–15%) for the "chaotic"/organic detune the brief asks for.

(C) The FM excitation spectrum (the "aggressive/metallic" part). Non-integer c:m ratios produce inharmonic sideband sets (Chowning); ratios above ~1:3 give clangorous metallic percussion, and bell/clang FM canonically uses non-integer ratios (e.g. 1:1.4 Chowning bell, 5:7 for bells). At fmRatio 0.6 → c:m = 1:2.8, a strongly inharmonic excitation: the carrier+sidebands hit the membrane as a metallic transient. High mod-index at high velocity (up to 3.0 rad) widens the sideband fan → louder hits are brighter/clangier (velocity→index is the classic FM-drum brightness law).

(D) Secondary metallic body / coupling. Real kicks get "snap"/"beater" character and (in 808/909-style designs) a second tonal layer above the body. Here the brief's "metallic secondary body (0.60/0.85)" = a coupled secondary SHELL bank (post-audit feedforward+passive topology) tuned smaller (secondarySize 0.60 → shell f0 ≈ 0.55×head f0) and metallic (secondaryMaterial 0.85 → low b3, long bright highs). It adds an inharmonic metallic ring on top of the membrane sub — the "metallic coupling" the brief calls for, and a key differentiator from a clean 808.

(E) Attack click / contact transient. Kick "click" energy lives ~3–8 kHz (beater contact). I keep the always-on Click layer moderate and fairly bright so the FM clang has a defined leading edge, but not so hot that it masks the FM character (the FM burst already supplies most of the aggressive transient).

(F) Noise content. The brief specifies a noise layer at 0.25 — a modest broadband bed (kick noise is mostly low-mid "air"/beater scuff, not snare hiss). I keep it brown/pink-ish and low-passed, short decay, so it thickens the transient/air without turning the kick into a snare.

(G) Decay / T60. Kick body decay is short-to-medium (808 center ~300 ms; tighter synthetic kicks 100–250 ms). I voice a moderately tight body (Decay ~0.42 → ~0.9× of the membrane base, giving roughly a 150–250 ms ring at this material/size) so the sub has weight but the kick stays punchy and percussive, not a drone.

VALUES THAT DRIVE PARAM CHOICES: fundamental glide 300→30 Hz; c:m 1:2.8 inharmonic FM; membrane Bessel ratios 1.59/2.14/2.30/2.65; click band 3–8 kHz; body T60 ~150–250 ms; secondary shell ~0.55×f0 metallic; velocity→FM-index 0.5→3.0 rad.

archetype: Frame Drum (world) — large thin single-headed hand frame drum (tar / bodhrán / daf / riq family)
bodyModel: Membrane (Bessel circular-membrane modal bank, 48 modes) — the only body that consumes airLoading and tensionMod, and the only physically correct model for a struck circular skin.
exciter: Mallet (softer beater/"tipper" contact) — the broad, low-frequency, soft-tipped excitation a frame-drum beater or fingertip delivers, weighting the low diametric modes rather than splashing energy into the high modes (which a hard Impulse would).

params.rationale: Soft beater/tipper/fingertip contact; weights the low diametric modes for a broad low tone instead of a hard high-mode splash.
The only physically correct model for a struck circular skin, and the only body that consumes airLoading + tensionMod.

params.physicalTarget: brightness≈0.32; baseDecayTime=lerp(0.15,0.8,0.32)*(1+0.1*size)≈0.36 s; low high-mode brightness → woody, fast high-mode decay
params.rationale: Thin natural skin on a wood frame is woody, not metallic; low brightness lets the highs die first so the broad low (0,1) tone dominates.
Large thin head; 79 Hz lands in the measured 18-22 inch frame-drum / tar register (E2, B1-A2 range).

params.physicalTarget: decayTime = baseDecayTime*exp(lerp(ln0.3,ln3,0.42))≈0.36*0.83≈0.30 s body T60 on the fundamental
params.rationale: Short-to-medium sustain: shallow/open-backed, hand-damped skin blooms then dies in a few hundred ms (no sealed-kettle ring).

params.physicalTarget: r/a = 0.32*0.9 = 0.29 → near-center-to-mid strike; |J_m(j_mn*r/a)| weights the (0,1)/low modes
params.rationale: Frame drum 'tone'/bass voice is struck toward the center; this keeps the (0,1) fundamental dominant rather than splashing edge modes.
A low frame-drum hit needs headroom to read at musical loudness alongside other pads; just under unity.

params.physicalTarget: high air loading: lower Bessel modes depressed toward the Rossing preferred set 1:1.5:2:2.44:2.9
params.rationale: THE defining frame-drum lever — pulls the low diametric modes down for a deep, definite, 'less whistly' pitch dominated by the air-loaded (0,1) mode.
A skin membrane should keep its physical Bessel/air-loaded spacing; no added stiffness inharmonicity (that is a bar/plate trait).

params.physicalTarget: phys = 0.62*2-1 = +0.24 → tilt energy/decay toward the LOW modes (highs decay faster, skewBias shortens global decay slightly)
params.rationale: Reproduces the frame drum's fast-dying highs over a lingering broad low tone — the (0,1)-dominated sustain the archetype calls for.

params.physicalTarget: ~1.8% sinusoidal frequency dither on the modal set
params.rationale: Natural-skin heads are never perfectly uniform; a touch of scatter avoids a sterile single-pitch ring without detuning the fundamental.

params.physicalTarget: b3 = 0.55e-3 → moderate f^2 (frequency-squared) damping: highs die well before the fundamental
params.rationale: Woody skin/frame: the high diametric modes are damped faster than the low ones, leaving the broad low tone (overrides the material-derived b3 for a deterministic woody decay).

params.physicalTarget: clamp(0.12)*3.3e-5 energy scale → small upward energy-dependent pitch bump (well under +1 semitone), relaxing as the head decays
params.rationale: Thin hand-drum heads show a subtle quasistatic tension 'kerthump' (tension ∝ membrane energy); kept light so it adds life without a tom-like glide.

params.physicalTarget: light always-on contact transient: mix 0.38*vel*env into output + half into exciter feed
params.rationale: A soft beater/tipper contact 'clicky' attack defines the hit; light, not a stick crack.
Soft beater contact time — a touch longer than a hard stick tick to keep the attack rounded.
Dull-to-mid 'thud/clop' contact, not a sharp metallic tick — matches a wooden tipper on skin.

params.physicalTarget: light parallel noise layer at mix 0.1 (well under the body)
params.rationale: Frame drums have only faint skin/beater contact + breath noise; no snare wires. Keep it as a subtle realism dusting.
Contact/breath noise sits low-mid, matching the skin's dark broadband attack, not a bright hiss.
The contact-noise burst is brief — it belongs to the attack, not the sustain.
Skin/beater/air contact noise is dark and low-tilted, not white hiss.

params.physicalTarget: 0.25 participation in the global cross-pad sympathetic engine (audible only if kit-level coupling > 0)
params.rationale: 'Light coupling' per the archetype: a small sympathetic weight so multiple frame-drum hits breathe together, without snare-buzz-level participation.

defaultedParams.why: ToneShaper left transparent (cutoff norm 1.0 = bypass, env amount 0.5 = no mod): the body+airLoading already shapes the tone; no post-filter coloration is part of a natural frame-drum sound.
A clean acoustic skin has no saturation/wavefolding character; both stages bypass at 0 to keep the body's modal spectrum intact.
PitchEnv Time = 0 disables the whole 808-style sweep; a frame drum has no swept boom — its only pitch motion is the subtle energy-driven tensionMod, which is the correct membrane mechanism.
Left at 0 (exact bypass): a synthetic integer-harmonic series would overwrite the air-loaded near-harmonic (but not exactly integer) membrane spectrum that defines the instrument.
A thin frame head is essentially linear at musical velocities; amplitude-driven brightening belongs to cymbals/gongs, not a quiet hand drum. Exact bypass at 0.
No intra-hit timbre sweep in a frame-drum strike; the material is static, so Material Morph stays off.
Frame-drum hits ring out and overlap naturally; no open/closed mute-group relationship to assign.
Routed to the main bus (0); no multi-out separation needed for a single world-percussion voice.

defaultedParams.param: FM Ratio (32) / Feedback Amount (33) / NoiseBurst Duration (34) / Friction Pressure (35)
defaultedParams.why: No-ops for the Mallet exciter — these only affect FMImpulse/Feedback/NoiseBurst/Friction respectively, so they stay at their inert defaults.
Left neutral at 0.5 (zero delta): the recipe sets the underlying material/decay/size/skew/coupling explicitly, so the one-knob macros must not add hidden offsets.
Left at the -1 sentinel (derive from the Decay knob): Decay 0.42 already gives the wanted ~0.3 s fundamental T60, so no flat-damping override is needed.

Head<->shell coupling is off: a frame drum has only a thin rim, not a resonant shell/kettle, so there is no secondary body to couple into (distinct from the light global Coupling Amount kept at 0.25).
Center (0.5): default stereo placement for a lead world-percussion voice; per-kit positioning is a mix decision, not an instrument property.
On (1.0): this is a sounding pad.

physics: PITCH / FUNDAMENTAL: A frame drum's pitch is set almost entirely by head diameter (Cooperman: each diameter has a narrow tuning range). Measured ranges — 22": B1–D2 (~62–73 Hz); 20": C2–E2 (~65–82 Hz); 18" tar: E2–A2 (~82–110 Hz, "as low as C, as high as F"); 16": A2–C3 (~110–131 Hz); bodhrán sits in the low D–G register. Target a LARGE thin head: f0 ≈ 78–85 Hz (≈ E2). In Membrum, Membrane base = 500 Hz, f0 = 500·0.1^size, so size 0.80 → 79.1 Hz, size 0.78 → 83.2 Hz — dead-on the tar/large-frame register. MODE STRUCTURE / INHARMONICITY: an ideal (unloaded) circular membrane has inharmonic Bessel ratios 1 : 1.59 : 2.14 : 2.30 : 2.65 : 2.92 (Rossing/A&S). AIR LOADING (the mass of surrounding air moving with the head) drops the LOWER modes substantially while barely touching the high modes, pulling the diametric series toward the near-harmonic "preferred" set 1 : 1.5 : 2 : 2.44 : 2.9 (Rossing 1982; Well-Tempered Timpani). This is what gives a frame drum its DEFINITE, deep, "less whistly" low pitch instead of a vague Bessel splash. A center-struck single-headed frame drum is dominated by the (0,1) fundamental; edge/rim strikes bring up higher diametric modes. So: high airLoading + center-ish strike + the (0,1) mode carrying the tone. DECAY / T60: short. Unlike timpani (sealed kettle → strong air resonance, long ring), a frame drum is shallow/open-backed and HAND-DAMPED at the rim and back, with goatskin's tight-but-flexible fibre giving "sharp attack, controlled sustain." Sustain is short-to-medium and broad-band: the low tone blooms then dies in a few hundred ms; the back hand mutes it further. Target body T60 ≈ 0.25–0.5 s on the fundamental, with the highs damped faster (woody material). TRANSIENT / CLICK: a soft beater ("tipper") or fingertip — "clicky" but soft (Hot-Rod/brush-like), NOT a stick crack. A light contact click defines the attack without a metallic tick. NOISE: light — skin/beater contact and a little breath/air; low-mid spectral tilt (brown/pink), short, well under the body. NO snare/wire content. PITCH GLIDE: hand drums show a small energy-dependent tension "kerthump" (Avanzini–Marogna–Bank 2012: quasistatic tension ∝ membrane energy) — present but subtle on a thin frame head; keep light or off. MATERIAL: thin natural skin over a wood frame → "woody," not metallic: low brightness, moderate frequency-squared (b3) damping so the highs die before the fundamental, leaving the broad low tone.

archetype: Friction Membrane Drone (drone) — rubbed/bowed-membrane sustained drone, the membrane analog of the string drone
bodyModel: Membrane (offset 1 = 0.0 → BodyModelType::Membrane). Bessel circular drumhead, 48 modes, air-loadable. Chosen over String because the brief wants the *membrane* analog: an inharmonic, air-loaded, low-fundamental resonator that is darker and rounder than the harmonic waveguide String, and the only body on which tensionModAmt (the cuíca-style pitch glide) is active.
exciter: Friction (offset 0 = 0.6 → ExciterType::Friction; idx=round(0.6*5)=3). BowExciter in transient struck-bow mode: stick-slip rosin junction drives the body with a sawtooth-like (Helmholtz) force — all integer harmonics at 1/n / -6 dB/oct — which is precisely the bowed/rubbed-membrane excitation of a cuíca/friction drum. frictionPressure (offset 35) biases bow pressure heavier for a thicker scrape.

params.rationale: Stick-slip friction is the defining excitation of a rubbed-membrane friction drum; gives the sawtooth/Helmholtz drive.
params.citation: Friction drum / cuíca stick-slip → sawtooth-like drive (Wikipedia/Grokipedia Friction drum & Cuíca; Bart Hopkin)

params.rationale: The membrane analog of the string drone: inharmonic, air-loadable, low-fundamental, darker/rounder; only body where tensionMod is active.
params.citation: Vibrations of a circular membrane (Wikipedia); Rossing air loading (wtt.pauken.org)

params.physicalTarget: membrane brightness=0.42; baseDecayTime=lerp(0.15,0.8,0.42)*(1+0.1*size)≈0.42 s; mild inherent stretch
params.rationale: Mid-low material = woody/skin, darker-than-string tone; keeps brightness modest for the round drone (b3 override then sets the real HF roll-off).
Low bass-tom/drone register, the growling low resonance of a friction drum; set lower/darker than the alternating String drone's pitch.
params.citation: friction drums emphasize low-frequency 'growling resonance' (Grokipedia Friction drum)

params.physicalTarget: decayTime≈base*exp(lerp(ln0.3,ln3,0.92))*skewBias ≈ 2.7× base → long ring (feeds legacy law; b1 override governs flat damping)
params.rationale: A drone must sustain; longest musical ring so the membrane keeps singing under the bow.
params.citation: matches the string-drone sibling decay 0.92 (droneSustainKit) — sustained-drone design

params.physicalTarget: r/a=0.4*0.9=0.36 → off-center Bessel sampling; excites diametric (m,1) modes
params.rationale: Cuíca rod sits off-center/toward the middle; off-center coupling brings in the inharmonic diametric modes for the vocal growl.
params.citation: cuíca rod attached toward membrane center; pressing nearer center raises pitch (BYU/Grokipedia Cuíca)

params.rationale: Matches the drone-kit per-pad level; leaves headroom for the sustained, energy-building drone under the -1 dBTP bus limiter.
A lowpass shapes the darker/rounder drone, rolling scrape highs off the sustained tone.
Low cutoff darkens the drone to a round tone; filter env then opens it as the note swells.
params.citation: string-drone sibling cutoff 0.45 (droneSustainKit) — matched for the alternating pair

params.rationale: Modest resonant emphasis at the cutoff adds a vocal formant-like peak (the cuíca's vowel-like quality) without self-oscillation.

params.physicalTarget: amount=0.55*2-1=+0.10 → cutoff sweeps up ~+0.3 oct on env
params.rationale: Slight upward sweep brightens as the bow energy builds (the swell of a drone); keeps filter engaged.
Quick but not instant filter onset — the scrape eases in rather than clicking.
params.citation: friction onset is a soft scrape, no hard transient (Bart Hopkin)

params.rationale: Initial brightness settles toward the sustain over a tenth of a second.
High sustain keeps the filter partly open for the duration of the drone — a SUSTAINED filter ADSR, not a percussive pluck.
Long filter release lets the drone fade smoothly when released.
Membrane is already inharmonic via Bessel+air-loading; keep stretch near physical so the natural drumhead spectrum dominates.
params.citation: membrane inharmonicity is intrinsic (Bessel) — no extra stretch needed; droneSustainKit ~0.30-0.36

params.physicalTarget: phys=0.85*2-1=+0.70 → skewBias=1-0.3*0.85≈0.745; per-mode tilt ratio^(-0.70) favors low modes
params.rationale: Tilts spectral/decay energy toward the LOW modes — the dark, round, low-dominant growl of a friction drum; high partials decay faster.

params.physicalTarget: amount 0.18 → adds 8 integer harmonics at 1/k (-6 dB/oct) on f0
params.rationale: Small dose reinforces the bowed sawtooth (1/n) harmonic series the friction drive physically produces, adding tonal body/fundamental weight to the drone.
params.citation: stick-slip drive = sawtooth, all integer harmonics ∝1/n (handwiki Sawtooth; Helmholtz motion JASA)

params.physicalTarget: amount 0.45 → drive=1+6*vel*0.45*env; louder→brighter odd-harmonic waveshaping, sustained
params.rationale: Friction excitation is strongly nonlinear (non-harmonic overtones, amplitude-dependent brightening); harder bowing brightens the drone dynamically.
params.citation: friction stick-slip is nonlinear, amplitude-dependent (Grokipedia Friction drum); audit M-3/M-4 redesign

params.rationale: A drone wants a firm, steady rub; extra pressure thickens the scrape and richens the harmonic content.
params.citation: higher normal force → longer stick phase → more harmonics (McGill bowed-string thesis); droneSustainKit pressure 0.35-0.59

params.physicalTarget: feedforward shell drive 0.18 (engages since Secondary on)
params.rationale: Adds a coupled shell/resonator-box layer — the friction drum's sound box that amplifies the growl.
params.citation: resonator box couples to membrane, amplifies low growl (Grokipedia Friction drum); droneSustainKit 0.18

params.rationale: The friction drum is membrane + resonant body box; the secondary bank is that coupled box.
params.citation: friction drum = membrane over a sound box (Wikipedia Friction drum)

params.rationale: Box resonance below the head (Chromaphone ~0.6 rule) reinforces the low growl.

params.physicalTarget: shell b1=max(25,2.5*b1)+(1-0.65)*15; b3=0.65e-5 → brighter, longer-ringing box
params.rationale: A wood/metal can resonator rings a bit brighter and longer than the damped skin, adding sustain body.

params.physicalTarget: effective=0.4*3.3e-5*energy; up to ~+2 semitone energy-dependent upward glide (Membrane-only)
params.rationale: The cuíca's signature tension/energy-driven pitch bend — the drone swells and bends pitch upward as energy builds.
params.citation: cuíca pitch varies with membrane tension over ~2 octaves (BYU/Grokipedia Cuíca); Avanzini-Marogna-Bank 2012

params.rationale: Stick-slip rosin jitter adds low scrape/breath noise; kept modest so the tonal body dominates the drone.
params.citation: rosin jitter / friction junction noise (FrictionExciter; Grokipedia stick-slip)

params.rationale: Dark scrape noise, matched to the round low drone — no bright hiss.
params.citation: darker, rounder target (task brief); droneSustainKit noiseCutoff 0.50

params.rationale: Pink (−3 dB/oct) is the natural broadband scrape tilt, dark and full, not violet/white sizzle.
Long noise decay sustains the scrape through the drone rather than a percussive burst.
Slight emphasis, no whistling peak on the lowpass scrape noise.
params.citation: default; low-Q to avoid pitched-beep (noise_layer LP)

params.rationale: A rubbed membrane has no beater/contact click — the onset is a soft scrape.
params.citation: friction onset is a continuous rub, no struck transient (Bart Hopkin); droneSustainKit clickMix 0.0

params.rationale: Overrides the long-Decay flat damping with a moderate floor so the sustained body rings cleanly without an infinite/ringing tail; seeds the shell b1 too.
Rolls off the high modes for the 'darker, rounder' membrane tone (skin/wood, not metal).
params.citation: darker/rounder target (task brief); Chaigne&Askenfelt b3 material law (audit §3-A); droneSustainKit b3≈0.18-0.28

params.physicalTarget: each membrane mode freq scaled 1+0.5*(targetScale-1) → half-way to Rossing air-loaded series
params.rationale: Air loading depresses the lowest Bessel modes toward the near-octave timpani series, deepening and rounding the membrane (less whistly) — the round drone.
params.citation: Rossing air loading lowers low modes, near-octave ratios (wtt.pauken.org Air Loading)

params.rationale: A real rubbed skin is imperfect/wobbly; small scatter adds the 'humanly imperfect, wobbly' organic detune of friction tone.
params.citation: 'wobbly, humanly imperfect' friction timbre (Bart Hopkin); droneSustainKit scatter 0.10-0.34

params.physicalTarget: strong participation in global sympathetic-resonance engine
params.rationale: Drone kit runs globalCoupling 0.85 / tomResonance 0.65; high per-pad weight lets the membrane drones ring sympathetically with each other.
One-knob lift toward a richer, more coupled, more harmonically alive drone (matches the kit's macro voicing).
Drone sits centered; per-pad pan spreads the 8 drone voices across the stereo field at kit-build time (alternating L/R), but the archetype baseline is center.
params.citation: audit M-9 per-pad pan; center default

defaultedParams.why: NonlinearCoupling already supplies the amplitude-dependent harmonic brightening; a second saturator would muddy the round drone — left bypassed (0).
Wavefolding is a west-coast aggressive timbre, antithetical to a dark/round friction drone — off (0).
Pitch motion here is the energy-driven tensionMod glide (the physically-correct cuíca mechanism), not a fixed 808-style pitch-env; PitchEnv Time=0 keeps the whole pitch-env feature disabled.

Material Morph is an optional per-note timbre sweep; the drone's timbre evolution comes from the sustained filter ADSR + tensionMod + nonlinear coupling, so morph is left off for this baseline (the kit may enable it on a subset).
No-op unless FMImpulse exciter is selected; we use Friction.
No-op unless Feedback exciter is selected; we use Friction.
No-op unless NoiseBurst exciter is selected; we use Friction.
A sustained drone should never choke or be choked — 0 (no choke).
Routing is a kit-layout decision (the kit alternates drone pads across aux buses); archetype baseline is main bus (0).
No-op because Click Mix=0 (no beater click on a rubbed membrane).

defaultedParams.param: Tightness / Brightness / Body Size / Punch macros (off 37,38,39,40)
defaultedParams.why: Left neutral (0.5) so the explicit per-param voicing above is preserved; only Complexity is dialed for the kit's richer-coupling character.
On (1.0) — the pad must sound.

physics: FUNDAMENTAL/PITCH: Friction membrane drums (cuíca, rommelpot, lion's roar) are low-pitched growling instruments; the resonator emphasizes low-frequency components ("growling resonance"). The cuíca's pitch is set by tension on the membrane/rod and is swept over UP TO TWO OCTAVES by pressing the head (increased tension → higher f0). Membrum membrane f0 = 500*0.1^size Hz; size≈0.50 → ~158 Hz fundamental (low tom / bass-drone register), deliberately lower/darker than the alternating String drone. DRIVING WAVEFORM: stick-slip (Helmholtz) friction produces a near-sawtooth periodic drive — spectrum contains ALL integer harmonics with amplitude ∝ 1/n (-6 dB/oct) (confirmed sawtooth Fourier series; bowed-string Helmholtz motion). This is the same -6 dB/oct slope the audit set for mode_inject (1/k), so a small mode_inject reinforces the physically-correct bowed harmonic series. PARTIAL/MODE STRUCTURE & INHARMONICITY: a circular membrane is intrinsically INHARMONIC (Bessel zeros, not integer ratios); air loading (Rossing/timpani) depresses the lowest diametric modes (1,1),(2,1)… toward the near-octave timpani series 1:1.5:2:2.44:2.9, giving a deeper, less-whistly, rounder tone. The friction drive then couples into and excites those inharmonic membrane modes (non-harmonic overtones, "vocal-like"/growling timbre per the cuíca/friction-drum literature). DECAY/T60: this is a SUSTAINED drone, not a struck transient — the perceptual decay is long; we set Decay≈0.92 (≈3× base, the longest musical ring) and use a moderate flat-damping floor (bodyDampingB1) so the body rings under the bow rather than dying. The bow's own transient envelope (A1/D40/S0/R5 ms) repeatedly re-excites; long body decay + sustained filter ADSR carry the drone. ATTACK TRANSIENT/CLICK: a rubbed membrane has NO hard beater click — onset is a soft scrape, so Click layer is muted (clickLayerMix=0). NOISE CONTENT: stick-slip rosin jitter adds broadband scrape/breath noise, low and dark — a modest brown/pink noise layer at low cutoff. PITCH GLIDE: the cuíca's signature is energy/tension-dependent pitch bend; tensionModAmt (Membrane-only) reproduces the energy-dependent upward glide (Avanzini-Marogna-Bank 2012: tension ∝ membrane energy), set moderate for an expressive, swelling drone. MATERIAL: a rubber/skin membrane over a wood/can resonator → mid-low material (woody-to-neutral), brightness from material, with b3 frequency-squared damping rolling off the highs for the "darker, rounder" target.

archetype: Friction String Drone (bowed/rub-stick sustained string drone — modular west-coast drone + tanpura-style sustained drone)
bodyModel: String (Krate::DSP::WaveguideString — Extended Karplus-Strong / Smith digital waveguide). Chosen because the defining sound is a sustained, harmonically-rich PITCHED string with a 1/n (sawtooth/Helmholtz) spectrum and a slowly-evolving, decade-long ring — exactly what the single-loop waveguide produces and what a modal bank does NOT (the modal bodies give struck-plate/bell/membrane decays, not a continuous string drone). String also bypasses the shared modal bank, so modeStretch/decaySkew/modeInject/modeScatter/airLoading and tensionMod are inherent no-ops here; the drone's inharmonicity and brightness come from the waveguide loss filter (driven by Material) rather than a stretch knob.
exciter: Friction (FrictionExciter → Krate::DSP::BowExciter, transient struck-bow / stick-slip junction, McIntyre-Schumacher-Woodhouse-style friction). It injects a short (≤46 ms) rosin-jitter stick-slip excitation that kicks the waveguide into a Helmholtz-like motion; the sustained DRONE itself is then the long waveguide decay (T60 ≈ 5 s), not continuous bowing (sustained bowing is out of Phase-2 scope). frictionPressure biases the bow harder for a brighter, scrapier attack. This is the same family as a bowed-string physical model where the bow is the nonlinear scattering junction feeding a waveguide.

params.rationale: The friction/rub-stick exciter is the defining excitation; round(norm*5) with stepCount 5 must land on index 3.
params.citation: euphonics.org/11-3-0 (Helmholtz stick-slip); Smith digital-waveguide bowed string

params.rationale: A sustained pitched string drone needs the waveguide, not a struck modal body; index 3 selects String.

params.physicalTarget: gut/nylon-ish: waveguide brightness_=1-0.5=0.5 (moderate loop-filter damping); T60 base=lerp(0.5,4.0,0.5)=2.25 s
params.rationale: Mid material = bright-but-not-glassy harmonic tail and a long sustain base, matching a bowed gut/nylon drone string.
Clearly-pitched low/mid drone in the tanpura/modular-drone register, leaving headroom for upper-partial evolution; not so low it turns to mud.
Archetype calls for very long decay 0.85-0.95; ~5 s sustain is a genuine drone, and high harmonics naturally die first so the tone evolves.
params.citation: Darbar 'The Tanpura: One Note Wonder' (multi-second ring); Jivari (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jivari)

params.physicalTarget: pickPosition clamp(0.13,0.02,0.98)=0.13 → comb null near 1/0.13≈8th harmonic region; bridge-ish contact
params.rationale: A near-bridge bow/contact point (sul ponticello-ish) keeps low harmonics strong and avoids deep mid comb-nulls, giving a full drone; matches the exciter's own 0.13 bow position.
A sustained always-on drone is energetically dense; trim below the percussive default 0.8 so stacked drone notes share headroom under the -1 dBTP bus limiter.
params.citation: AUDIT H-1/H-2 gain-staging (body at -12 dBFS, bus TruePeakLimiter)

params.rationale: A gentle lowpass on the ToneShaper tames the bow's upper-mid scrape and shapes the evolving brightness, the natural choice for a warm drone.
params.citation: ResearchGate Violinist's Sound Palette (bow force = brightness)

params.rationale: Engages the lowpass (below 20 kHz so it is NOT bypassed) just above the fundamental's first few harmonics to round off bow noise while the filter envelope opens it; central to the slow timbral evolution.
params.citation: euphonics 11-3-0 (high harmonics carry the bow brightness)

params.rationale: A moderate resonance gives a vocal/formant lift at the moving cutoff — the 'breathing' overtone emphasis of a jivari/west-coast drone.

params.physicalTarget: amount = 0.62*2-1 = +0.24 → up to 2^(3*0.24)≈+0.7 oct upward sweep
params.rationale: A slow UPWARD filter sweep makes the drone brighten then settle — the evolving-texture signature of west-coast drones; bipolar 0.5 would be no-op.
A non-instant attack lets the brightness bloom IN like a bow digging in, not a percussive snap.
Slow decay toward the sustain level so the brightness change is audible as evolution, not a transient.
Holds the filter partway open through the long drone so the tone stays alive rather than closing to silence.
Gentle release matches the long string tail so the filter doesn't snap shut before the body has decayed.
params.citation: derived from T60≈5 s drone tail

params.physicalTarget: internalDrive = 1+0.18*9 = 2.62; reciprocal-sqrt waveshaper with unity small-signal makeup (flavour, not level)
params.rationale: A touch of odd-harmonic saturation adds rosin grit / west-coast warmth; post-M-2 makeup means it is TIMBRE not loudness.
params.citation: AUDIT M-2 (Drive = flavour with 1/internalDrive makeup); PsychoSynth (add-harmonics ethos)

params.physicalTarget: foldAmount = 0.22*pi ≈ 0.69 rad (Serge-style sine fold, wet/dry mix)
params.rationale: Light wavefolding is the literal west-coast drone move — adds shimmering upper harmonics that evolve with amplitude; kept low so it enriches rather than shreds the pitch.
params.citation: perfectcircuit.com 'What is West Coast Synthesis'; DAFx17 Buchla 259 wavefolder paper

params.rationale: Archetype names modeStretch, but the waveguide ignores it; left neutral so the value is harmless and the recipe is honest about it.

Archetype prescribes very high decaySkew 0.65-0.85; set to 0.75 for kit-consistency / macro behaviour, but flagged: String bypasses the modal bank so it does not tilt the spectrum here (the waveguide's own high-frequency loss already provides the 'highs die first' tilt).
params.citation: AUDIT M-5 (decaySkew is a modal-bank tilt); string_body ignores bank

params.rationale: The waveguide already produces the full 1/n harmonic series; injecting a second synthetic series would double the fundamental and muddy the drone.
params.citation: AUDIT §3-B mode_inject 1/k (the body already supplies this slope)

params.rationale: Amplitude-driven brightening mimics the bow's nonlinear stick-slip getting brighter with force and the jivari's amplitude-dependent buzz — the core 'evolving as it rings' character.
params.citation: AUDIT M-3/M-4 (env-level waveshaping); euphonics (bow force → brightness)

params.physicalTarget: >=0.5 → Material Morph ON (drives waveguide brightness/decay over the note)
params.rationale: A slow Material morph makes the loop-filter brightness/decay drift during the drone — the single most effective 'living drone' move on a String body.
params.citation: AUDIT M-1 (morph now drives String via refreshStringMaterial)

params.physicalTarget: Material 0.45 at onset → brightness_=0.55 (slightly darker/scrapier attack)
params.rationale: Begins a hair darker so the bow attack reads as a scrape, then opens.
Settles brighter/more even-harmonic as the drone establishes — the tanpura 'overtones bloom' effect.
params.citation: PureTones (sustained overtones emerge); string_mapper material→brightness invert

params.rationale: ~1.2 s morph spans the early evolution of the multi-second drone without being so slow it's inaudible.
A linear material glide reads as a smooth, even brightening; exponential would rush the change.
Drones are meant to overlap and sustain; choking would cut the sustained voices.
Archetype mandates outputBus 1 — route the sustained drone to its own pre-master bus for independent reverb/processing away from the percussive main mix.

params.physicalTarget: bow pressure = clamp(0.1+0.4*vel+0.45*0.5)= +0.225 bias → firm-ish bow
params.rationale: Sits in the archetype's 0.32-0.55 'moderate-to-firm bow' window; bow force is the dominant brightness control, and this keeps the attack scrapey without a raucous crunch (stays inside the Schelleng playable wedge).
params.citation: euphonics Schelleng (min/max bow force); ResearchGate Violinist's Sound Palette (bow force=centroid)

params.physicalTarget: 0.6 participation weight in the global sympathetic-resonance engine
params.rationale: Slightly above neutral so multiple drone strings sympathetically ring together (the tanpura's strings excite each other), audible only when the kit's Global Coupling/Tom-Resonance is up.

params.physicalTarget: 0.16 * env * lowpassed noise * 0.243 standalone gain → a faint breath bed
params.rationale: A quiet noise bed adds bow-rosin friction air and jivari shimmer-floor under the pitched string without becoming a hiss.
Keeps the noise dark and supportive (a low breath), not a bright hat sizzle.
params.citation: derived: friction air is broadband but should sit under the drone

params.rationale: Brown noise is the darkest source — a warm air bed that doesn't fight the string's own highs.
Long noise envelope so the breath/friction bed sustains with the drone rather than being a transient.
Archetype explicitly says NO click; a bowed onset is a scrape, not a beater contact.
params.citation: euphonics (gradual Helmholtz onset, no percussive contact)

params.rationale: Modal-frequency dither doesn't apply to the waveguide; kept at 0.

params.rationale: No coupled shell resonator for a bowed string.

No 808-style pitch glide on a sustained drone; disabling Time switches the whole pitch-env feature off.

Leave the one-knob macros neutral so the explicit param values above are what ship.

No attack-punch wanted on a bowed onset; neutral.

physics:
FUNDAMENTAL/PITCH: A drone sits on a single low-to-mid pitch. Tanpura Sa is typically male ~C-D (~110-150 Hz) and female a fifth up (~G-A ~190-220 Hz); analyzed tanpuras show Sa ~103 Hz and a Pa-sa-sa-Sa tuning where attention is on the SUSTAINED OVERTONES, not the fundamental (Wikipedia Tanpura; PureTones; ScienceDirect tanpura-drone study). West-coast modular drones are similarly low/mid evolving textures (PsychoSynth; Perfect Circuit). Target f0 ≈ 190 Hz (a low tenor drone — clearly pitched, room for upper-partial evolution).
PARTIAL/MODE STRUCTURE & INHARMONICITY: Steady bowed-string (Helmholtz) motion is very nearly periodic → a near-perfectly HARMONIC spectrum whose nth harmonic amplitude ∝ 1/n (an ideal sawtooth, i.e. -6 dB/oct), because the Helmholtz corner makes sudden direction changes that load the high harmonics (euphonics.org 11-3-0; Wikipedia Sawtooth wave; Standing Waves: Bows and Strings). This 1/n slope is exactly what the corrected Membrum mode_inject uses (audit §3-B: 1/k, -6 dB/oct), and what the waveguide's bright loss filter approximates. Real strings carry a small dispersive inharmonicity B≈1e-4 (nylon) to 2e-3 (steel) — modeled by the waveguide's optional dispersion allpass (stiffness), but StringMapper leaves stiffness=0, so the Membrum String is harmonic; "buzz/jivari" richness (tanpura) and bow scrape are instead carried by the loss-filter brightness + Friction attack. 
DECAY/T60: A drone must sustain for seconds. WaveguideString loss is rho = 10^(-3/(T60·f0)); StringMapper builds T60 = lerp(0.5,4.0,material)·exp(lerp(ln0.3,ln3.0,decay)). At material 0.5, decay 0.90 → T60 ≈ 5.4 s (a true drone); the tanpura literally rings for many seconds as energy slowly migrates across the jivari bridge (Darbar; Jivari Wikipedia). Higher harmonics decay faster than the fundamental (material/brightness sets the loop-filter high-frequency loss), so the drone darkens as it sustains — the characteristic evolving timbre.
ATTACK TRANSIENT / CONTACT: Bowed onset is a soft scrape, NOT a percussive click — there is a brief pre-Helmholtz transient of irregular slipping before periodic motion locks in (euphonics Schelleng/Guettler). So NO click layer; the Friction exciter's rosin-jitter stick-slip IS the attack. West-coast drones also evolve in rather than strike.
NOISE CONTENT: Bow/rub produces broadband friction noise and "rosin" hiss concentrated in the upper-mid band; jivari adds a buzzing high-overtone shimmer. A small, dark, long noise layer (brown/pink, low cutoff, long decay) sits under the string to add breath/air without becoming a hat.
PITCH GLIDE: None of the iconic 808 "kerthump" type. Steady bowing slightly FLATTENS pitch with bow force (anomalous low frequencies, pitch flattening — Violinist's Sound Palette), but that is a subtle, non-iconic effect; PitchEnv stays OFF. tensionMod is Membrane-only → inherent no-op for String.
MATERIAL: Steel/nylon/gut string under tension over a resonant body (tanpura: long gourd-bodied lute; bowed: violin-family or bowed-rod). Material ≈ 0.5 (gut/nylon-ish, moderately bright, not a glassy steel treble).
BOW-FORCE → BRIGHTNESS: Bow force is BY FAR the dominant control of spectral centroid — hard/fast bowing = strong high harmonics, light/slow = weak (ResearchGate Violinist's Sound Palette; euphonics). In Membrum the Friction exciter maps velocity AND frictionPressure into bow pressure (0.1 + 0.4·vel + 0.5·pressure) and into a velocity-driven bandpass centroid (400·20^vel Hz), so frictionPressure 0.32-0.55 is the "moderate-to-firm bow" sweet spot that the archetype prescribes.

archetype: Ghost Tone (high-stretch skeletal) — synthetic-fx, atmospheric inharmonic sustained sound design
bodyModel: Bell (BodyModelType=4) as the primary voice, alternating per-pad with String (BodyModelType=3). Bell gives the canonical inharmonic hum/prime/tierce(minor-3rd)/quint/nominal partial set (kBellRatios 0.25,0.5,0.6,0.75,1.0,...) — the melancholy, hollow, plaintive metallic skeleton; String (WaveguideString) supplies a parallel bowed/struck-string, bone-dry plucked-resonance character on the odd pads. Both are driven through a very high modeStretch on the Bell pads to push the partials far off their tuned ratios into a non-pitched, bone-like inharmonic cluster (String ignores modeStretch, so its skeletal character instead comes from Material-inverted loop damping + Friction bowing + long decay).
exciter: Cycles Mallet (1) / FMImpulse (4) / Impulse (0) with periodic Friction (3). Mallet = soft beater that excites low/mid bell partials gently (no hard click) → hollow tone. FMImpulse = Chowning bell-FM excitation (inharmonic c:m sidebands) layered onto the Bell body for the classic struck-bell clang. Impulse = a cleaner harder strike for variety. Friction (BowExciter, STK BowTable) = sustained bowed-metal/eerie-string excitation for the most atmospheric, evolving pads (bowed-cymbal/bowed-vibe glassy overtones). Secondary exciter params are only meaningful on the matching pads: fmRatio on FMImpulse pads, frictionPressure on Friction pads.

params.physicalTarget: Mallet (idx 1; round(0.2*5)=1). Per-pad the kit cycles Mallet/FMImpulse/Impulse with Friction on every 5th pad — Mallet norm 0.2, FMImpulse 0.8, Impulse 0.0, Friction 0.6.
params.rationale: Soft/inharmonic excitations (mallet, Chowning-FM, bow) produce hollow bell/clang tone without a percussive transient; Friction sustains the eerie bowed-metal texture.
params.citation: Chowning 1973 FM bell (high index, inharmonic c:m): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frequency_modulation_synthesis ; bowed-metal eerie/glassy overtones: https://soundiron.com/products/cymbology

params.physicalTarget: Bell (idx 4; round(0.8*5)=4). Even pads = Bell (0.8), odd pads = String (0.6 → idx 3).
params.rationale: Bell gives the inharmonic hum/prime/tierce/quint/nominal skeleton that reads as hollow and bone-like; String alternates a bowed/struck waveguide for variety.
params.citation: Bell partial set & inharmonic tierce: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strike_tone ; http://www.hibberts.co.uk/strike.htm

params.physicalTarget: Bell brightness=0.7+0.3*0.45=0.835, decay base=lerp(0.4,1.4,0.45)≈0.85 s. Range across pads 0.30–0.85 (skeletal pads kept on the lower, woodier/duller side of metallic).
params.rationale: Mid-low material keeps the bell metallic but not glassy-bright, giving a hollow bone timbre rather than a shiny chime; morph sweeps it for evolving texture.
params.citation: Bell mapper material formula (pad_config dictionary, body mapper)

params.physicalTarget: Bell f0=800*0.1^0.45≈284 Hz (String f0 same base). Kit spans size 0.20→0.75 → f0 ≈ 505 Hz down to ≈142 Hz.
params.rationale: Mid/low fundamentals put the inharmonic cluster in the eerie 140–500 Hz 'skeletal voice' register; lower modes also ring longer (weaker b3·f² damping).

params.physicalTarget: decayTime = baseDecay * exp(lerp(ln0.3,ln3.0,0.8)) ≈ base*1.93 → multi-second ring on the Bell. Kit 0.65–0.85.
params.rationale: Long sustained ring is the defining trait of an atmospheric ghost tone; the slowly-decaying low partials (hum) linger, as in real bells.
params.citation: Bell decay: hum lingers longest after strike: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strike_tone

params.physicalTarget: Bell: strike azimuth theta=0.3*(pi/2)≈27°; |cos(m*theta)| re-weights partials. String: pickPosition≈0.3 (comb notch).
params.rationale: Default-ish strike sits near a meridional antinode so the full inharmonic partial set is excited; varied lightly per pad for timbral spread.
Sustained inharmonic pads pile up energy under global coupling; a moderate per-pad level leaves headroom for the bus true-peak limiter and keeps the texture atmospheric, not loud.
params.citation: Post-audit gain staging: body strike −6 dBFS budget + −1 dBTP bus limiter (AUDIT §Appendix)

params.rationale: HP thins the body, removing low weight so the tone reads as skeletal/hollow/bone rather than full — a core archetype requirement.
A low-set HP corner scoops sub/low-mid body weight (skeletal), while leaving the inharmonic mid partials intact.
Mild emphasis at the HP corner adds a hollow formant edge without ringing the filter into a pitched whistle.

params.physicalTarget: amount = 0.45*2-1 = -0.10 (slight downward sweep, ≈ -0.3 oct over the env).
params.rationale: A small negative sweep lets the HP corner relax slightly into the tail, a subtle 'breathing' motion for an evolving atmospheric voice.
Short filter-env decay relative to the long body decay puts the tonal motion in the attack/early body, leaving the long tail static.

params.physicalTarget: physical = 0.5+0.77*1.5 = 1.655; bank B = stretchNorm*B_max = 0.77*0.01 = 0.0077, partials warp by sqrt(1+0.0077*(k+1)²) → strongly inharmonic. Kit per-pad 0.65→0.89 (B 0.0065→0.0089).
params.rationale: THE defining axis of this archetype: the most aggressive use of the widened B_max inharmonicity axis pushes the tuned bell partials far off-ratio into a non-pitched, bone-like inharmonic cluster. 0.65–0.89 is near-maximal stretch — comparable in spirit to large bass-string/struck-bar B values where the spectrum is audibly stretched.
params.citation: Corrected 1-indexed warp sqrt(1+B*(k+1)²), B_max=0.01 (AUDIT §3-B); stiff-string f_n=n·f0·sqrt(1+B·n²), real B up to ~2e-3 in piano bass: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inharmonicity ; https://arxiv.org/pdf/1603.05516

params.physicalTarget: phys = 0.81*2-1 = +0.62; skewBias=1-0.3*0.62=0.81 on global decay + per-mode tilt ratio^(-0.62) (clamped [1/8,8]). Kit 0.78–0.90 → phys +0.56…+0.80.
params.rationale: High positive skew tilts energy/decay toward the LOW inharmonic partials and lets the hum/prime linger — exactly the real-bell behaviour where low partials decay slowest, producing the hollow, settling, skeletal tail.
params.citation: Per-mode decaySkew tilt on all bodies (AUDIT M-5); bell hum lingers longest: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strike_tone

params.rationale: ModeInject adds an INTEGER-harmonic series (1/k) at f0 — the opposite of skeletal; it would re-pitch the voice and fight the inharmonicity. Deliberately off.
params.citation: ModeInject = 8 integer harmonics 1/k, zero-leak bypass at 0 (mode_inject.h, AUDIT)

params.physicalTarget: amount 0.32 → env-level-driven waveshaping drive=1+6*vel*0.32*env; louder→brighter, sustained. Kit 0.20–0.44.
params.rationale: Adds a gentle amplitude-driven brightening that animates the long tail (mimics nonlinear plate/shell coupling in struck metal) without compressing or homogenizing — keeps the sustained tone alive.
A per-note Material morph makes each ghost tone evolve in timbre over its long sustain — essential to an atmospheric/sound-design voice rather than a static hit.
params.citation: materialMorph sweeps Material into the active mapper (AUDIT M-1, all bodies)

params.rationale: Begins duller/woodier so the morph opens toward brighter metal across the tail.
Ends brighter/glassier, sweeping the inharmonic spectrum upward over the sustain for an evolving 'awakening' texture.
Long morph time matched to the long body decay so the timbral sweep spans the whole audible tail.
A linear material sweep gives a smooth, predictable evolution over the long sustain.
On FMImpulse pads, a non-integer c:m gives the inharmonic Chowning bell/clang spectrum; only meaningful on FMImpulse pads.

params.physicalTarget: bow pressure bias +0.5*0.3=+0.15 on Friction pads (effective clamp(0.1+0.4*vel+0.15)).
params.rationale: Moderate bow pressure on the Friction pads sustains the eerie bowed-metal scrape/tone without tipping into harsh squeal; no-op on non-Friction pads.
params.citation: FrictionExciter setPressure (STK BowTable); bowed-metal eerie texture: https://www.sonixinema.com/products/bowed-metals

params.physicalTarget: ~7% sinusoidal frequency dither on the modal partials. Kit 0.40–0.70.
params.rationale: Adds organic detuning/beating on top of the deterministic stretch so the inharmonic cluster shimmers and never sounds like a fixed chord — heightens the eerie, unstable character. No-op on String pads.

params.physicalTarget: 0 — no air-loading frequency correction (Bell/String ignore it anyway).
params.rationale: Air loading is a Membrane-only timpani correction; meaningless for Bell/String here and left off so the bell ratios stay pure-then-stretched.

params.physicalTarget: mix 0.12 → quiet parallel filtered-noise layer (≈ -18 dBFS-class). Kit 0.10–0.40.
params.rationale: A faint dark noise bed adds breath/air under the bell tone for atmosphere, kept low so the inharmonic body stays the focus.
Low cutoff keeps the noise dark and unobtrusive — a hollow draft, not hiss.
Brown noise gives the darkest spectral tilt for a subterranean atmospheric bed under the skeletal tone.

params.physicalTarget: ms = exp(ln20 + 0.85*(ln2000-ln20)) ≈ 1100 ms long noise tail.
params.rationale: Long noise decay sustains the airy bed across the whole ghost-tone ring rather than cutting at the attack.

params.physicalTarget: mix 0.12 → faint attack click (also half-amp into exciter feed). Kit 0.10–0.34.
params.rationale: A whisper of contact click gives the soft mallet/strike a faint point of articulation; kept low so attacks stay soft and hollow (bowed/atmospheric pads tend toward 0).
Mid-bright click adds a subtle bone-tap edge without a sharp metallic tick.

params.physicalTarget: b1 = 0.2 + 0.35*49.8 ≈ 17.6 s^-1 flat damping (overrides Decay-derived flat damping). Kit 0.30–0.40.
params.rationale: A low flat-damping floor gives a long overall RT60 so the inharmonic tail rings out for atmosphere. No-op on String pads.
params.citation: bodyDampingB1 0.2..50 s^-1; low = long tail (membrane_mapper dampingLawFromParams)

params.physicalTarget: b3 = 0 → pure flat damping, NO f²-dependent high-mode damping (metallic, highs ring long).
params.rationale: Bell metal sustains its high inharmonic partials; zeroing b3 keeps the upper partials alive for a glassy, long-ringing metallic tail (this is why the kit sets b3=0 on Bell pads). String pads use ~0.18 (no-op anyway).
params.citation: b3=0 = metallic long highs (AUDIT body damping; kit Bell pads set b3=0)

params.physicalTarget: 0 = off (and it is Membrane-only, so it is a no-op on Bell/String).
params.rationale: Energy-dependent tom 'kerthump' glide is Membrane-gated and irrelevant to a sustained inharmonic bell/string voice; off.

params.physicalTarget: 0.85 per-pad weight into the GLOBAL cross-pad sympathetic engine (audible because the kit sets globalCoupling≈0.78 + tomResonance≈0.55).
params.rationale: High inter-pad sympathetic coupling makes the skeletal voices ring each other — a haunted, self-resonating ensemble that thickens the atmosphere.
params.citation: couplingAmount → global sympathetic engine (Phase 5; kit globalCoupling 0.78)

params.rationale: Spreading some pads to an aux bus gives the requested output-bus spread for separate reverb/space processing of the atmospheric layers.

params.physicalTarget: Center (equal-power, unity both channels) as the per-pad neutral; spread across pads ~0.25–0.75 for a wide stereo image.
params.rationale: Per-pad panning widens the inharmonic cluster across the stereo field for an enveloping atmospheric image (the kit relies on global coupling + aux spread; individual pads can be spread L–R).
Sustained, overlapping atmospheric tones must ring freely and layer — choke groups would cut the tails. Off.

params.physicalTarget: ON for the ~14 crafted skeletal pads; uncrafted pads disabled by the generator.
params.rationale: Kit crafts a focused set of ghost-tone voices; remaining pads disabled so re-enabling is an explicit gesture.

defaultedParams.why: Bypassed at 0; ReciprocalSqrt warmth would thicken/odd-harmonic the tone, fighting the clean hollow inharmonic skeleton. Flavour not wanted here.
Bypassed at 0; wavefolding adds aggressive west-coast harmonics — the opposite of a sparse skeletal spectrum.
PitchEnv Time=0 disables the whole feature; a ghost tone is a static-pitch sustained drone, not an 808-style glide — no per-hit pitch sweep wanted.
Filter-env motion is intentionally minimal (small amount, short decay); attack≈0, sustain≈0, default releases keep the subtle sweep confined to the attack.
No-op unless the Feedback exciter is selected; this archetype uses Mallet/FMImpulse/Impulse/Friction, so it stays at default on every pad.
No-op unless NoiseBurst exciter selected; not used in this archetype (left at default 0.5).
The faint dark noise bed needs no spectral peak; default low Q keeps it a smooth airy draft, not a pitched hum.
Click is barely present (mix ~0.12); the default 2–5 ms contact is fine and not a meaningful lever for a soft/atmospheric attack.

Macros are neutral one-knob deltas; the recipe sets the underlying params directly. Brightness/Complexity are nudged up modestly per-pad in the kit (≈0.30–0.85) but the baseline is the neutral 0.5 so the explicit param values define the sound. Punch/Tightness left neutral — no transient punch or tightening wanted on a sustained drone.

defaultedParams.param: Secondary Enabled / Coupling Strength / Secondary Size / Secondary Material (54-57)
defaultedParams.why: Per-pad head↔shell coupling is mostly off for these voices (sustained inharmonic bell/string needs no added shell housing); a minority of pads enable it lightly for body weight, but the archetype baseline leaves it at default-off so the inharmonic body stands alone.

physics: FUNDAMENTAL/PITCH: This archetype has no single clear pitch by design. Bell body f0 = 800·0.1^size; the kit's size span 0.20–0.75 puts f0 ≈ 505→142 Hz (baseline ≈284 Hz at size 0.45), the eerie low-mid 'skeletal voice' register. PARTIAL STRUCTURE & INHARMONICITY: A real bell's named partials are hum (0.5× prime / octave below), prime (1.0), tierce (1.2, a MINOR THIRD — inharmonic, gives bells their plaintive/melancholy character), quint (1.5), nominal (2.0), twelfth (3.0), upper octave (4.0); Membrum's kBellRatios encode this with the hum at 0.25·f0_nominal (0.25,0.5,0.6,0.75,1.0,...). The archetype then deliberately DESTROYS the tuning with very high modeStretch: norm 0.65–0.89 → bank B = 0.0065–0.0089 (B_max=0.01), warping each partial by sqrt(1+B·(k+1)²) so the upper partials stretch sharp into a non-pitched bone-like inharmonic cluster — the most aggressive use of the corrected, widened B inharmonicity axis. This mirrors the stiff-string law f_n = n·f0·sqrt(1+B·n²) used for pianos/bars where real bass B reaches ~2e-3; Membrum's B_max=0.01 is several× that for an exaggerated 'unnatural' stretch. modeScatter (~7%) adds organic detune/beating so the cluster shimmers. String pads instead use the WaveguideString (no modal stretch) for a parallel bowed/struck plucked-resonance skeleton. DECAY/T60: long. Bell base decay ≈0.85 s × Decay multiplier (norm 0.8 → ×1.93) and a low flat-damping b1 (≈17 s^-1) with b3=0 (no f² high-mode damping) → multi-second ring where, per the literature, the low hum/prime partials linger longest and the high inharmonic partials sustain (metallic) rather than dying fast; high positive decaySkew (+0.56…+0.80) tilts decay/energy toward the low partials to reproduce that 'hum lingers' settling tail. TRANSIENT/CLICK: minimal — soft Mallet beater + a faint click (mix ~0.12), or no transient at all on bowed (Friction) pads, so attacks are hollow, not percussive. NOISE: a quiet, dark Brown-noise bed (mix ~0.12, ~320 Hz LP, ~1.1 s decay) supplies atmospheric air/breath under the tone. PITCH GLIDE: none (PitchEnv disabled) — a ghost tone holds pitch; the only motion is the slow per-note Material morph (≈1.4 s, 0.45→0.80) sweeping the inharmonic spectrum brighter over the sustain, plus amplitude-driven NonlinearCoupling brightening. MATERIAL: bronze/metal (Bell) and bowed metal/string — the eerie 'glassy/plaintive' overtone character that bowed-cymbal and bowed-vibe libraries are prized for in horror/atmospheric scoring.

bodyModel: Bell (BodyModelType::Bell, body offset 1 norm 0.8 → enum idx 4). The Bell body is the only Membrum resonator built on the tuned church-bell / inharmonic-metal partial series (16 Chladni partials, hum at 0.25·f0, nominal at 1.0, with a long low-b3 metallic ring). It is the correct skeleton for any "ringing glass / tuned bell" timbre. Plate would give a denser cymbal-like Chladni cloud (no clear pitched partials), Shell a glockenspiel bar (too few, too even partials); Bell gives the clean+slightly-detuned pitched-glass character the archetype wants.
exciter: Cycled per i across three exciters, all of which leave the Bell body audible and pitched: FMImpulse (default, i%3!=0 and not friction) — a Chowning bell-FM transient that injects an inharmonic metallic attack chime whose c:m ratio is set per-pad by fmRatio; Mallet (i%3==0) — a soft beater giving the cleanest "tap=pure bell" hits; Friction (i%4==3) — a struck-bow transient adding a scrape/shimmer onset to a minority of pads for variation. The exciter only shapes the ATTACK/excitation; the sustained pitched ring is the Bell body. FMImpulse is the signature glass-bell exciter here because its non-integer modulator ratio reinforces the body's inharmonicity at the strike (Chowning 1973: c/m≈1/1.4 → inharmonic bell spectrum).

params.physicalTarget: i-cycled: FMImpulse (norm 0.8 → idx 4, the default for i%3!=0 & i%4!=3), Mallet (norm 0.2 → idx 1, i%3==0), Friction (norm 0.6 → idx 3, i%4==3). Representative baseline = FMImpulse.
params.rationale: FMImpulse's non-integer modulator ratio reinforces the Bell body's inharmonic attack (Chowning bell-FM); Mallet gives the cleanest pure taps; Friction adds shimmer variety. All keep the body pitched.
params.citation: Chowning 1973, 'Synthesis of Complex Audio Spectra by FM' (c/m≈1/1.4 → inharmonic bell).

params.physicalTarget: idx 4 = Bell (church-bell Chladni partials 0.25/0.5/0.6/0.75/1.0/1.5/2.0/2.6…, hum at 0.25·f0, 16 modes).
params.rationale: Only body with a tuned/inharmonic bell partial series and a long low-b3 metallic ring — the glass-bell skeleton.

params.physicalTarget: i-ramp 0.40→0.95 across the 16 pads; baseline ~0.6. Bell brightness=0.7+0.3·0.6=0.88 (very metallic), baseDecayTime=lerp(0.4,1.4,0.6)=1.0 s.
params.rationale: Glass/bell bronze is a hard low-loss material → high brightness, long bright tail; the i-ramp tilts the garden from warmer to brighter glass.
params.citation: Avant Acoustics: lead crystal = low damping/long pure ring.

params.physicalTarget: i-ramp 0.10→0.70 (small→larger), reversed vs i (size=0.10+((15-i)/15)·0.60); baseline mid ~0.46 → nominal f0=800·0.1^0.46≈277 Hz, with nominal partial + upper partials reaching ~1–3 kHz; small pads (size 0.10) f0≈630 Hz.
params.rationale: Puts the bell garden in the glass register (musical strike pitches C4–C6); larger pads ring lower/longer, small pads ping high — one skeleton scaled across pitch.
params.citation: Jundt 2006 / PDX singing-glass fundamentals ~530–700 Hz; glass-harmonica range.

params.physicalTarget: i-ramp 0.55→0.90 (0.55+(i%6)·0.07); baseline ~0.62 → multiplier exp(lerp(ln0.3,ln3,0.62))≈1.06×, so decayTime≈baseDecayTime·1.06 ≈ ~1 s+ for the longer pads.
params.rationale: Glass/bells ring long; high decay gives the sustained glassy tail. Note bodyDampingB1 (offset 50) is set, so this mainly feeds the cache/legacy fallback while b1 sets the actual floor.
params.citation: Hibberts: bell low partials ring for seconds.

params.physicalTarget: linear 0.68 pre-rail attenuation, flat across pads.
params.rationale: Bells are resonant/peaky; modest level leaves bus-limiter headroom for the 16-voice polyphonic garden (maxPoly 16).
params.citation: Audit gain-staging appendix: −6 dBFS body budget + bus limiter owns the sum.

params.physicalTarget: default 0.3 → bell strike azimuth θ=0.3·π/2≈0.47 rad; weights partials by |cos(m·θ)|.
params.rationale: Left at the natural clapper-on-soundbow default; a near-antinode strike that excites all partials cleanly.

params.physicalTarget: i-ramp 0.30→~0.95 (0.30+fmod(i·0.04,0.65)); baseline 0.30 → modulatorRatio=1.0+3.0·0.30=1.9; i=2 gives ~0.38→2.14; classic glassy bell-FM sits near 1.4–2.0.
params.rationale: FMImpulse-only: non-integer c:m makes the attack inharmonic/metallic; ramping it detunes the chime across the garden. No-op on Mallet/Friction pads.
params.citation: Chowning 1973: c/m≈1/1.4 inharmonic bell; √2≈1.414 classic bell ratio.

params.physicalTarget: i-ramp 0.55→0.79 (0.55+(i%4)·0.08); baseline ~0.63 → phys=2·0.63−1=+0.26. Per-mode tilt ratio^(−0.26): cuts upper partials' sustain, boosts hum.
params.rationale: Positive skew = upper partials decay faster than the hum — the measured glass/bell decay law (strike tone dies first, hum rings on).
params.citation: Hibberts: high partials high-intensity/short, hum lowest/longest.

params.physicalTarget: i-ramp 0.30→0.66 (0.30+(i%7)·0.06); baseline ~0.42 → phys=0.5+0.42·1.5≈1.13 (mildly stretched), stretchNorm≈0.42 into the bank → slight sharpening of upper partials.
params.rationale: Real struck glass is INHARMONIC (2nd mode ~2.3–2.6× not 2×); a touch of positive stretch detunes the partials sharp for the 'slightly detuned glass' character.

params.physicalTarget: i-ramp 0.30→0.62 (0.30+(i%5)·0.08); baseline ~0.46 → ~7% sinusoidal frequency dither on the modal partials.
params.rationale: Organic per-partial detune gives the natural imperfection / faint beating of real glass (no two glasses identical).
params.citation: Singing-wineglass doublet beats (Perrin doublets; arXiv phase-locking wineglasses).

params.rationale: Hard glass strikes brighten slightly with velocity (louder=brighter); kept small so quiet taps stay pure-bell.
params.citation: Stowell cymbal 'tap=bell, hard=broadband'; audit M-3/M-4 redesign.

params.physicalTarget: 0.0 → b3=0 (pure flat damping, no f² high-freq loss) → very long upper-partial ring, maximally metallic/glassy.
params.rationale: Glass/bell-bronze has very low high-frequency internal loss → bright long highs; b3≈0 overrides the material-derived woody damping.
params.citation: Avant Acoustics lead-crystal high-Q; bell_mapper FR-024 'very low b3'.

params.physicalTarget: i-ramp 0.30→0.54 (0.30+(i%7)·0.04); baseline ~0.40 → b1=0.2+0.40·49.8≈20 s⁻¹ flat damping floor (moderate-long ring).
params.rationale: Sets the overall RT60 floor directly (overrides Decay's flat-damping derivation); modest b1 = a long but not infinite bell tail.
params.citation: bell_mapper dampingLaw; Hibberts multi-second bell decay.

params.physicalTarget: i-gated: ON (1.0) for i%3==0, OFF (0.0) otherwise; baseline (FMImpulse pad) = OFF.
params.rationale: On a third of pads a coupled secondary shell adds a metallic body resonance under the bell; off elsewhere keeps the pure glass tone.
params.citation: Chromaphone-style coupled resonator; audit M-6 shell stage.

params.physicalTarget: i-gated 0.25 for i%3==0 else 0.0; baseline 0.0 (no head→shell drive on FMImpulse pads).
params.rationale: Drives the secondary shell only where it is enabled; 0 elsewhere = no-op.

params.physicalTarget: flat 0.85 → bright, long-highs metallic shell (shell b3=0.85·1e-5, low b1 add).
params.rationale: The 'metallic secondary (0.85)' from the archetype: a bright bell-metal shell where coupling is engaged.
Shell sits a perfect-fifth-ish below the bell for a coupled-body weight where enabled.

params.physicalTarget: i-ramp 0.55→0.85 (0.55+(i%4)·0.10); baseline ~0.55 → moderate participation in the kit-level sympathetic-resonance engine (globalCoupling 0.65).
params.rationale: The 'couplingAmount' of the archetype: pads ring each other sympathetically, building a resonant glass garden.
params.citation: audit Phase-5 cross-pad coupling; globalCoupling kit opt.

params.physicalTarget: i-ramp 0.32→0.56 (0.32+(i%3)·0.12); baseline ~0.32 → modest bright contact tick.
params.rationale: Glass/clapper contact is a short bright 'tink' — present but not dominant.
params.citation: Hibberts strike-tone transient; glass tap attack.

params.rationale: Bright tick suits a hard glass strike (sharp not dull).
Very short, light contact = brittle glass tap.
Struck glass has almost no broadband content; just a faint air/sizzle.
High cutoff = bright thin air, not a dark thud.
Bright/violet-white noise matches a glassy sizzle.
A short-medium airy tail trailing the strike.
params.citation: glass air decays with the body.

params.physicalTarget: i-gated: 0.30+(i%4)·0.10 on Friction pads (i%4==3), else 0.0; baseline (FMImpulse pad) 0.0.
params.rationale: Sets bow pressure on the minority Friction pads for a scrape-shimmer onset; no-op elsewhere.

params.physicalTarget: i-ramp 0.0→0.32 ((i%5)·0.08) on non-Friction pads, 0 on Friction; baseline 0.0. No-op (no Feedback exciter selected).
params.rationale: Set by the i-ramp but inert — FM Ratio/Feedback/Friction are exciter-specific and only the matching one is live per pad.

params.physicalTarget: i-gated ON for i%5==0 (every 5th pad), OFF otherwise; baseline OFF.
params.rationale: A few pads sweep material (timbre brightens through the note) for variety; most stay static-pure.

params.physicalTarget: on morph pads: start 0.40→0.70, end 0.85; baseline values present but inert when morph OFF.
params.rationale: Material climbs (darker→brighter) over the note where morph is on.

params.physicalTarget: i-ramp 0.55→0.95 (0.55+(i/15)·0.40); baseline ~0.55 → opens ToneShaper filter + adds modeInject across the garden.
params.rationale: One-knob brighten ramp tilts the whole garden brighter with i (glass gets more crystalline up the range).
Mild complexity ramp thickens the sympathetic glass garden.
Air loading is a Membrane frequency correction; set to 0 explicitly since it is meaningless for Bell.
Left centered (the generator does not spread pan here); a stereo spread across the 16 pads would be a reasonable enhancement but is not part of this archetype's spec.

params.physicalTarget: i-gated: bus 1 for i%8==7 (norm≈0.0667→idx1), else main (0).
params.rationale: A couple of pads route to an aux for separate processing; rest on main.

defaultedParams.why: A clean tuned bell/glass is a linear resonator with NO pitch glide; PitchEnv Time=0 disables the whole 808-style sweep family.
Energy-driven pitch glide is Membrane-ONLY (gated on body type); a no-op on the Bell body, so left at the harmless i-ramp/0 default.
ToneShaper filter left bypassed (cutoff norm 1.0 = transparent, env amount 0.5 = none) — the Bell body + macroBrightness already shape the spectrum; an extra SVF would muddy the pure glass tone.
Saturation/wavefolding add gritty harmonics antithetical to a clean glassy bell; left at 0 (exact bypass).

Left 0 explicitly — the Bell body already supplies the tuned partial series; an added synthetic 1/k harmonic stack would dull the inharmonic glass character (and macroBrightness/Complexity can nudge it where wanted).
Glass-bell garden pads should ring freely and overlap (resonant garden); no mute-grouping.
Left neutral 0.5 — material/size/decay/decaySkew are already set explicitly per pad, so these one-knob proxies add nothing and 0.5 = zero delta (preserves the crafted values).
Noise layer is a thin background air; default low resonance (0.2) is fine, no spectral peak wanted.
All 16 crafted pads on (1.0); the other 16 GM slots disabled by the kit's crafted list.

physics:
GLASS-BELL / STRUCK-GLASS ACOUSTICS (researched).
Fundamental/pitch: a struck wine glass / glass bell rings in the low-kHz region; measured singing-glass fundamentals span ~530–700 Hz, with glass-harmonica/struck-glass musical range roughly C4–C6 (~260 Hz to ~1 kHz). Membrum's Bell body f0 = 800·0.1^size gives 80–800 Hz; for glass we want the upper half of that (small bodies, size ~0.10–0.45 → f0 ≈ 800→285 Hz nominal partial), with the higher partials (×2, ×2.6, ×3.2…) carrying the bright glassy "ping" up into 1–3 kHz. (Jundt et al. 2006 UNSW; PDXScholar singing-wine-glass Fourier study.)
Partial/mode structure & inharmonicity: glass and bells are genuinely 2-D shells, modes labelled (m meridional nodal lines, n nodal circles); lowest acoustically important mode is (2,0). For a WINE GLASS the fundamental:second-mode ratio is measured ~2.3–2.6 (601.5/1427.5 Hz =1:2.37; 700/1765 =1:2.52; 529.3/1368 =1:2.58) — strongly INHARMONIC, NOT a 1:2 octave. A tuned CHURCH BELL is engineered to ~hum:prime:tierce:quint:nominal = 0.25:0.50:0.60:0.75:1.00 (=1:2:2.4:3:4 above hum) — Membrum's kBellRatios encodes exactly this (0.25,0.5,0.6,0.75,1.0,1.5,2.0,2.6,3.2,…). An untuned struck glass sits between these: pitched but with a prominent slightly-sharp "second mode" and audible beating. Membrum reaches the "slightly-detuned glass" zone via modeStretch (>neutral stretches partials sharp, stiff-bar dispersion √(1+B(k+1)²)) + modeScatter (organic detune dither) + the FMImpulse inharmonic attack. (Perrin/Charnley/DePont 1983 JSV; Rossing Science of Percussion Ch.5; Hibberts; Jundt 2006.)
Per-band decay / T60: HIGHER partials decay FASTER than lower ones — the strike tone (nominal/quint, high intensity, short) dissipates first, the hum (lowest, lowest intensity, longest) rings on for seconds. Crystal/lead glass has very low damping (high Q) → long pure ring; ordinary glass damps faster (impurities/amorphous structure broaden the response). Bell/large-glass T60 ranges from sub-second up to several seconds. In Membrum this is: long base decay (Bell baseDecayTime lerp(0.4,1.4,material)·(1+0.1·size)) × the Decay-knob 0.3–3.0× multiplier; the "highs die first" tilt is the per-mode decaySkew tilt ratio^(-skew) (positive skew → faster-decaying upper partials = realistic glass), and the bright-metal long-highs come from very low b3 (bodyDampingB3≈0 = pure flat damping, long upper partials). (Hibberts "musical sound quality of church bells"; Avant Acoustics on lead-crystal Q.)
Attack transient / contact click: a clean mallet/clapper contact is a short bright tick (a few ms); for glass it is a light "tink". Modeled by the always-on ClickLayer (2–5 ms bandpassed burst, bright center) at modest mix plus, on FMImpulse pads, the Chowning bell-FM attack chime itself.
Noise content: minimal — a struck glass has almost no broadband noise; just the brief contact tick and a hint of air. So the parallel NoiseLayer is kept LOW and BRIGHT (violet/white, high cutoff) — a thin sizzle, not a snare hiss.
Pitch glide: a clean bell/glass has essentially NO pitch glide (it is a tuned linear resonator); only very large bells/heavily-struck glass show mild amplitude-dependent effects. So the 808-style pitch envelope is OFF (PitchEnv Time 0); a faint energy-driven brightening is supplied instead by NonlinearCoupling, and tensionModAmt is a no-op on Bell (Membrane-only) so it stays at the i-ramp default harmlessly.
Material: hard, brittle, high-stiffness amorphous solid (silica glass) / bell bronze — both metallic, low internal loss → Membrum material kept HIGH (brightness 0.7+0.3·material, b3 low) for the glassy metallic tail.

params.rationale: A gong is struck with a soft padded mallet, not a hard stick — broad soft contact that dumps energy low and lets the bloom rise; brief specifies Mallet.
params.citation: Fletcher & Rossing, Physics of Musical Instruments ch.20 (gong excited by soft beater)

params.physicalTarget: idx=round(0.8*5)=4 → Bell body (16 inharmonic Chladni partials, hum at 0.25·f0)
params.rationale: Bell gives a deep inharmonic metal spectrum with a sub-octave hum and the gong's low fifth (prime→quint); the only Membrum body that natively voices a tuned-yet-inharmonic struck-metal column.
params.citation: Rossing, Science of Percussion ch.5; brief

params.physicalTarget: Bell brightness=0.7+0.3·0.85≈0.955 (very metallic, weak f²-damping → long bright highs); decay base lerp(0.4,1.4,0.85)≈1.25 s

params.rationale: Tam-tams sustain for many seconds to minutes; this is the longest practical ring for the bloom and the low fifth tail.

params.rationale: A gong is a loud, dominant instrument; sits hot but under the −6 dBFS body budget + bus limiter post-audit gain staging.
