defaultedParams.param: Coupling Strength / Secondary Enabled / Secondary Size / Secondary Material
defaultedParams.why: Head↔shell coupling is a drum-shell concept (kicks/toms); a cymbal has no shell resonator, so secondary stays off.
Membrane-only (gated); a NoiseBody plate ignores it — no tom-style tension glide on a cymbal.
Macros left neutral 0.5 (zero delta) so the explicitly-voiced base params are preserved; only Brightness is nudged.

physics: SPLASH ACOUSTICS (researched): A splash is the smallest cymbal of the crash family — 8" is the most common diameter (range 4"–13") — a thin bronze plate with FREE edges. Like all cymbals it is strongly INHARMONIC: >100 (up to ~300 in a 16") normal modes designated (m nodal diameters, n nodal circles), with modal frequencies following the modified Chladni power law f = C·(m+2n)^P, where P for cymbals/bells ranges 1.4–2.4 (Rossing, Am.J.Phys. 1982; classical flat-plate value is 2). The lowest cymbal modes sit roughly 150–400 Hz for larger cymbals; an 8" splash is pushed higher and, more importantly, the mode density is so high above ~1–3 kHz that the spectrum is heard as a bright noise-like quasi-continuum rather than discrete tuned partials. PER-BAND DECAY: cymbals decay fastest in the highs, slowest in the lows; for a SPLASH the whole sustain is "extremely short, usually less than a second" (Wikipedia) — 2–4 s figures cited for splash are generous; the practical voiced T60 is ~0.3–0.8 s, vs 8–15 s for a crash, and "smaller cymbal = higher pitch = faster decay." ATTACK / NONLINEAR CASCADE: a struck cymbal does NOT present its full spectrum instantly — most strike energy first activates LOW modes, then nonlinear mode-coupling cascades energy UP into a dense high-frequency cluster over the first tens-to-hundreds of ms ("rapid increase in high-frequency energy, subharmonic generation, chaotic noise" at large amplitude — Fletcher & Rossing; Schedin/Gren/Rossing transient holography). For a splash this build is very fast (brief), giving the instant bright "splash." NOISE CONTENT: the first moments of a cymbal hit "sound a lot like white noise" (Stowell); the established synthesis is filtered noise driving a large bank of inharmonic resonators — Stowell explicitly gives a SPLASH band of 3,500–20,000 Hz with a brief noise build, vs 300–20,000 Hz for a crash and 150 Hz+/20-s decay for a gong. No deterministic pitch glide (cymbals have no tom-style "kerthump"; any pitch change is the amplitude-dependent nonlinear effect, not a tuned glide). Material is bronze (B8/B20) — metallic, long-ringing highs intrinsically, deliberately damped short here by size+decay. MAPPING DRIVERS: Membrum NoiseBody f0 = 1500·0.1^size; at size 0.22 → ~903 Hz fundamental, plate-ratios fanning up into the kHz inharmonic cluster, with the internal noise layer cutoff = clamp(1500+5000·material) and the parallel NoiseLayer carrying the bright hiss. The "small + short" splash is reached by low size (high pitch), low decay (fast T60), fast noise decay, very bright noise color, and outputBus 1 to keep cymbals on their own send.

archetype: Sub-Bell Perc (modular) — west-coast tuned FM-bell percussion
bodyModel: Bell (church-bell Chladni partial bank, 16 modes, Simpson true-harmonic ratios hum 0.25 / prime 0.5 / tierce 0.6 / quint 0.75 / nominal 1.0; f0_nominal = 800·0.1^size Hz)
exciter: FMImpulse (Chowning bell-FM operator pair; modulator:carrier ratio = 1.0 + 3.0·fmRatio; mod-index env decays faster than amp env for the bright→pure metallic attack)

params.rationale: Chowning FM-bell operator pair gives the inharmonic metallic strike with a bright→pure attack (mod-index env decays faster than amp env).

Church-bell Chladni partial bank (Simpson ratios hum0.25/prime0.5/tierce0.6/quint0.75/nominal1.0) IS a tuned bell; the FM exciter drives these modes for a true bell resonance, not just a synthetic FM tone.

params.physicalTarget: Bell brightness = 0.7+0.3·0.6 = 0.88 (very metallic); base decay = lerp(0.4,1.4,0.6)=1.0 s before Decay/skew
params.rationale: Cast-bronze/steel bell: high brightness keeps b3 (f² damping) low so upper partials ring; mid material centers the body decay near 1 s.
A LOW 'sub' tuned bell — nominal in the low-mid where the strike pitch reads as a bass perc note, with the hum two octaves down giving sub weight.
'Medium decay' tuned-perc hit (~0.7 s ring), not a long cathedral bell; long enough for the low partials to sing, short enough to be percussive.

params.physicalTarget: strike azimuth θ=0.2·(π/2)=0.31 rad; |cos(m·θ)| weights partials, near a meridional antinode
params.rationale: Clapper near the soundbow antinode excites the full partial set strongly (bright, even balance) as a hard clapper would.

params.physicalTarget: linear voice gain 0.8 before the transparent hardClip rail
params.rationale: Default per-pad loudness; FM-bell + body is hot, leave headroom for the bus limiter.

params.physicalTarget: modulator:carrier ratio = 1.0+3.0·0.72 = 3.16 (highly inharmonic, far from the tame 1.4 Chowning bell)
params.rationale: High non-integer ratio yields a dense clangorous inharmonic FM spectrum — the gritty west-coast metallic timbre rather than a clean cast bell.

params.physicalTarget: physical = 0.5+0.45·1.5 = 1.175 (mild upward stretch); stretchNorm fed to bank ≈ 0.45
params.rationale: Pushes the upper bell partials slightly off the just Simpson ratios for a rougher, more clangorous alloy/odd-geometry bell — the 'evolving metallic' grit, without losing the bell identity.

params.physicalTarget: phys = 0.42·2-1 = -0.16 (slight negative skew); per-mode tilt ratio^(+0.16) lifts upper partials, global skewBias = 1-0.3·(-0.16)=1.048 (slightly longer)
params.rationale: Real bells: low partials ring long, high partials are intense but short. A small negative skew adds upper-partial shimmer/perc bite while keeping the sub hum/prime as the tail.

params.physicalTarget: env-level waveshaping: drive = 1+6·vel·0.4·env; louder/early = more odd harmonics, mellowing as the body decays
params.rationale: Delivers the 'feedback/gritty/evolving' character the plan wants (since FMImpulse cannot use operator feedback) via the audit-correct amplitude-driven brightening that mimics nonlinear bell-shell coupling.

params.physicalTarget: internalDrive = 1+0.3·9 = 3.7; ReciprocalSqrt waveshaper with unity-makeup (M-2) → adds odd harmonics, not level
params.rationale: West-coast waveshaping warmth/grit on the bell tone; flavour not loudness after the audit M-2 makeup.

params.physicalTarget: always-on attack transient at 0.5·vel·env, half into the exciter feed + full to output (·0.445 standalone gain)
params.rationale: The metallic clapper/mallet 'ting' contact transient that defines a bell/metallophone attack.
Bright, sharp metallic tick (hard clapper on cast metal) rather than a dull thud.
Very short hard contact — a crisp metallic ping, not a soft beater.

params.physicalTarget: parallel noise layer at 0.12·env·filteredNoise (·0.243 standalone gain) — quiet
params.rationale: Cast-bell tone is highly tonal; only a touch of metallic air/sizzle, well below the body.
Keeps the sliver of noise high/airy (metallic shimmer) rather than rumbly.
Bright metallic hiss to match the high-metal character.
Short airy decay so the noise is an attack/early sizzle, not a sustained bed.
Tuned lead-perc element sits centered by default; per-kit placement can move it.

params.physicalTarget: no membrane air-loading frequency correction (Bell body ignores airLoading)
params.rationale: Air-loading is membrane-only; for a Bell body it is a structural no-op, so explicitly zero (not the 0.6 membrane default).

params.physicalTarget: sentinel −1.0 → derive flat damping from the Decay knob (1/decayTime)
params.rationale: Let the Decay knob set the ring; no need to override the flat damping for a tuned perc bell.

params.physicalTarget: sentinel −1.0 → derive f² damping from material/brightness (0.88 → very low b3)
params.rationale: The Material-derived metallic b3 (long upper partials) is exactly what a bell wants; no override needed.

defaultedParams.why: FMImpulse exciter hard-codes operator feedback to 0 and the param routes only to the FeedbackExciter (exciter_bank.h:144) — an exact no-op for this exciter, so the plan's 'feedback 0.30' is delivered via NonlinearCoupling+Drive instead.
No-op unless the NoiseBurst exciter is selected; FMImpulse is active here.
No-op unless the Friction exciter is selected.

defaultedParams.param: Filter Type / Cutoff / Resonance / Env Amount / Env ADSR (offsets 7-10,17-20)
defaultedParams.why: ToneShaper SVF left bypassed (cutoff default 1.0 = 20 kHz, env amount 0.5 = 0); the bell's metallic spectrum is already shaped by Material/b3 and the FM index, no post-filter needed.
Wavefolding left off; Drive already supplies the west-coast added-harmonic grit, and folding a tuned bell smears the pitch — kept clean.
Pitch-env Time=0 disables the whole glide; a bell holds its tuned strike pitch (no membrane kerthump), and the FM mod-index env already supplies the bright→pure spectral sweep.

Adds a synthetic integer-harmonic series; a bell is defiantly INHARMONIC (tierce 0.6, stretched upper partials), so injecting a harmonic series would fight the bell identity — left at 0 (exact bypass).
Cast bells are precisely tuned; left at 0 so the Simpson partial ratios stay exact (the deliberate inharmonicity comes from Mode Stretch, not random dither).
Material Morph off; the evolving timbre comes from NonlinearCoupling's amplitude-driven brightening, not a per-note material sweep — keeps the recipe focused.
Left at neutral 0.5 default; cross-pad sympathetic resonance is a kit-level decision, inaudible unless the kit's Global Coupling is engaged.

defaultedParams.param: Coupling Strength / Secondary Enabled / Secondary Size / Secondary Material (offsets 54-57)
defaultedParams.why: Head↔shell secondary resonator off; a single tuned bell needs no second coupled shell body, and the grit is already provided by NonlinearCoupling.
Gated to the Membrane body only — a structural no-op for the Bell body, so left at 0.
All left at neutral 0.5 (zero delta) so the explicit per-param values above are preserved exactly; macros are performance one-knobs, not part of the baseline voicing.
Routing defaults: 0 = no choke group, main bus — a tuned perc lead element is not a hat pair and needs no aux send by default.
Default 1.0 = enabled; the pad is part of the kit.
Left low (default 0.2); the quiet noise sliver needs no resonant peak, which would add a pitched beep.

physics: See structured physics field.

bodyModel: NoiseBody (plate-Chladni 32-mode modal bank + internal filtered-noise layer). NoiseBody is the only body that pairs the inharmonic free-plate (m+2n)^1.7 Chladni ratio table — the corrected cymbal/gong spectrum (audit §3-B, plate_modes.h) — with a built-in metallic noise field, exactly modelling a cymbal: a sparse set of low inharmonic partials (150-400 Hz fundamental) that thickens into a dense, noise-like sizzle above ~2 kHz. f0 = 1500·0.1^size.
exciter: NoiseBurst (violet-noise bandpass burst). A struck/rolled cymbal is excited broadband, not by a single clean click: the stick/mallet contact and especially the bow/roll deliver sustained high-passed noise energy that lights up the dense edge modes. NoiseBurst (offset 0 = 2) with a short contact for the closed hits and the always-on parallel Noise layer carrying the actual sizzle/swell.

params.rationale: Cymbals are excited broadband; the violet-noise burst lights up the dense edge modes far better than a clean click. Same exciter for all three variants.
params.citation: Fletcher & Rossing, Physics of Musical Instruments ch.20

params.rationale: Only body pairing the corrected inharmonic plate-Chladni ratio table with an internal noise field — the hybrid sparse-partials-below / dense-noise-above structure of a cymbal.

params.physicalTarget: brightness 0.7+0.3·0.95=0.985; internal noise cutoff clamp(1500+5000·0.95)=6250 Hz; noise reso 0.6+0.4·0.95=0.98; baseDecay lerp(0.2,1.0,0.95)≈0.96·(1+0.1·size)
params.rationale: Bronze plate: very metallic, low HF damping, bright sizzle. Near-max on all three variants.

params.physicalTarget: f0 = 1500·0.1^0.33 ≈ 700 Hz lowest-partial base; lowest Chladni (2,0)-family partial lands ~180-300 Hz audible
params.rationale: 16-18" orchestral suspended cymbal — fundamental in the 150-400 Hz band. Closed/pedal slightly smaller (0.30), roll variant larger (0.42) for a deeper swell.
CLOSED/short variant: a damped/choked short hit. PEDAL ≈ 0.10 (shortest). ROLL variant = 0.95 → baseDecay·~2.8 for the long 3-8 s low-mode swell.
params.citation: oemcymbal: edge T60 2-4 s, low modes 3-8 s vs HF <0.5 s

params.physicalTarget: edge/bow region of the plate (computePlateAmplitude cos(mθ)cos(nπρ) sweeps radius+azimuth)
params.rationale: Orchestral cymbals are struck/bowed near the EDGE, which excites high (m+2n) edge modes → bright shimmer. Roll variant 0.9 (bowed edge).
Cymbals are broadband and sit on an aux/perc bus; kept a touch under drum levels so the noise layer doesn't dominate the mix. Roll 0.58 (swells from soft).
params.citation: audit gain-staging appendix (-6 dBFS body budget)

params.rationale: A suspended cymbal's energy is HF-weighted; a gentle highpass thins the low-mid and emphasises the sizzle band.
Rolls off body weight below ~600 Hz, leaving the bright inharmonic shimmer. Engaged (not bypass) because filter type is HP.
No resonant emphasis wanted on a broadband HP; a flat corner.
The HF-darkening over time is carried physically by the modal/noise decay + decaySkew, not a synthetic filter sweep.
Drive is odd-harmonic flavour; the cymbal's broadband richness comes from the dense bank + noise, not saturation.
params.citation: audit M-2: Drive = flavour not level

params.rationale: West-coast wavefolding is not a cymbal mechanism.
Cymbals have no kerthump/pitch glide in the linear regime — the master enable stays off, which makes Start/End/Curve/Knee/Mid moot.
params.citation: Rossing & Fletcher, nonlinear gongs/cymbals (glide is amplitude-cascade, not pitch drop)

params.physicalTarget: physical = 0.5+0.7·1.5 = 1.55 → modes stretched ~55% beyond physical plate ratios → more inharmonic
params.rationale: Pushes partial spacing further off the ordered ratios toward a real cymbal's irregular inharmonic fingerprint.
params.citation: oemcymbal: inharmonicity coeff 0.3-0.8; audit stretch B_max widened

params.physicalTarget: phys = 2·0.66-1 = +0.32 → per-mode tilt ratio^(-0.32) boosts/sustains LOW modes, HF modes decay faster; global skewBias 1-0.3·0.32≈0.90
params.rationale: Reproduces the cymbal's time-variant darkening: bright at attack, HF sizzle dies first, low modes ring on. Stronger on the roll (0.55-0.66).
params.citation: oemcymbal: HF τ<0.5 s vs low modes 3-8 s

params.rationale: A synthetic 1/k harmonic series would impose tonal/pitched body — the opposite of a cymbal's inharmonic noise.

params.physicalTarget: amplitude-driven waveshaping: drive=1+6·vel·0.35·env → louder hits brighten/add broadband odd-harmonic energy
params.rationale: Directly models the cymbal energy cascade — harder strikes push energy up into a denser HF spectrum (chaotic regime). Roll variant 0.45.
params.citation: Touzé & Chaigne; Rossing & Fletcher nonlinear chaos in cymbals/gongs

params.rationale: Single short hits don't sweep timbre. ON (1.0) ONLY for the rolled/bowed swell variant.
Task requirement: choke 1 — a new hit (or a hand-grab) cuts the ringing cymbal, and the closed/pedal/roll mute each other like a hat pair.
Task requirement: the long rolled/swell variant routes to outputBus 1 for separate reverb/placement; short hits stay on main.
Short sharp contact for a struck closed hit. Roll variant longer (0.6 → ~9.8 ms) for a sustained bowed/rolled feel; the parallel Noise layer carries the actual swell.

params.physicalTarget: low participation in the global sympathetic-resonance engine
params.rationale: Cymbals shouldn't strongly drive/receive the cross-pad buzz network; kept low (no-op anyway unless kit Global Coupling>0).

params.physicalTarget: parallel filtered-noise layer at 0.62·env·noise → the dominant sizzle field
params.rationale: The radiated cymbal sound above ~2 kHz IS dense noise — this is the primary sonic layer. Roll variant 0.7.
params.citation: oemcymbal: noise-like dense spectrum above 2 kHz; F&R ch.20

params.rationale: Keeps the noise bright (sizzle band) while taming the harshest top. Closed slightly brighter (0.85), roll a touch warmer initially via the morph.
A real cymbal noise field has no single resonant peak; keep the lowpass nearly flat to avoid a pitched beep.
CLOSED short sizzle. PEDAL ≈ 0.1 (~25 ms). ROLL = 0.95 → ~1.7 s long swelling sizzle tail.
params.citation: oemcymbal: edge T60 2-4 s for sustained variant

params.rationale: Cymbal sizzle is bright/HF-weighted; violet noise (+6 dB/oct) matches the rising edge-mode density.
params.citation: oemcymbal: modal density ∝ f², edge-dominated >2 kHz

params.physicalTarget: low attack-transient level (mix·vel·env·noise; half into exciter feed)
params.rationale: A felt/bowed orchestral cymbal has a soft contact, not a hard tick. Small for closed, 0.0 for the bowed roll.
Brief soft contact; only audible when Click Mix>0.
What little contact exists is a high, thin tick consistent with a metal edge.

params.physicalTarget: b1 = 0.2+0.18·49.8 ≈ 9.2 s^-1 flat damping → long low-mode ring
params.rationale: Low flat-damping so the inharmonic low partials sustain (3-8 s class). Roll keeps it low (0.15-0.18); closed relies on Decay/noise envelope for shortness rather than a high floor so the body still rings briefly inharmonic.

params.physicalTarget: b3 = 0 → pure flat damping, NO frequency-squared roll-off → metallic long-ringing highs
params.rationale: Metal: high modes are NOT wood-damped; b3=0 keeps the bright sizzle alive (the HF-dies-first effect is delivered by decaySkew + the noise envelope, not material wood-damping).
params.citation: oemcymbal: bronze low intrinsic HF damping

params.physicalTarget: ~9% sinusoidal frequency dither on modal partials
params.rationale: Real cymbals have irregular, asymmetry-detuned partials; scatter breaks the ordered ratio grid into an organic inharmonic cloud. Roll 0.65.
params.citation: Rossing, normal modes of cymbals (asymmetric/detuned modes)

params.rationale: No tom kerthump; and tensionMod is gated to Membrane bodies so it would do nothing on NoiseBody regardless.

params.physicalTarget: theta=0.62·π/2; gainL=√2·cos≈1.12, gainR=√2·sin≈1.22 → slightly right of center
params.rationale: Orchestral suspended cymbal sits stage-right/percussion-section off-center; a gentle offset opens the stereo image. Roll could mirror left (0.40) if paired.

params.physicalTarget: Material value 0.55 at note start (ROLL only)
params.rationale: Roll begins darker/duller (less HF), then brightens as the swell builds — models the cymbal energy cascade lighting up edge modes as the roll intensifies.
params.citation: Touzé & Chaigne energy cascade; task spec morph 0.55->0.95

params.rationale: Swell peak: full shimmer. Task-specified 0.55->0.95 sweep (ROLL only).
A slow ~1.7 s crescendo matching an orchestral roll/swell (ROLL only).

params.physicalTarget: <0.5 → linear lerp (false; exp threshold not crossed) — gradual brightening
params.rationale: A smooth, near-linear brightening over the roll (ROLL only).

defaultedParams.why: Filter Env Amount is 0 (no sweep), so the filter ADSR is inaudible — left at defaults.
PitchEnv Time = 0 disables the whole pitch envelope; cymbals have no pitch glide, so every pitch-env breakpoint is a no-op.
FMImpulse exciter not selected — FM ratio is a no-op for NoiseBurst.
Feedback exciter not selected — no-op.
Friction exciter not selected — no-op (a true bowed cymbal could use Friction, but NoiseBurst+Noise-layer swell is the cleaner, choke-friendly model here).
Left neutral 0.5 — the recipe sets the underlying material/decay/cutoff/scatter directly, so macro deltas would just double-count; neutral preserves the explicit preset values.

defaultedParams.param: Coupling Strength / Secondary Enabled / Secondary Size / Secondary Material (54,55,56,57)
defaultedParams.why: Secondary shell disabled (0) — a freely-suspended cymbal is a single plate with no head/shell coupling; the secondary bank would add an unphysical second resonator.
Membrane-only frequency correction; a no-op on NoiseBody (kept at default, ignored by the plate/noise mapper).
1.0 (on) — these are active hat-slot pads.

defaultedParams.param: Filter Resonance (9) / Noise Resonance kept low rather than defaulted
defaultedParams.why: Explicitly set low (not left at struct default) because a resonant peak on a broadband cymbal would create a pitched beep — flagged but intentionally minimal, not defaulted.

physics: FUNDAMENTAL/PITCH: A suspended cymbal has NO definite pitch. Lowest normal mode (2,0 type) lands ~150-400 Hz depending on diameter/thickness (Mosico/Arborea modal data; Fletcher & Rossing "Physics of Musical Instruments" ch.20). I target the low end (≈180-260 Hz fundamental) for an 16-18" orchestral suspended cymbal → Size norm ≈ 0.30-0.36 gives f0 = 1500·0.1^0.33 ≈ 327-377 Hz for the bank's lowest partial; the perceptually-lowest audible energy sits below that because the (2,0)/(3,0) family ratios in the Chladni table start near unity. PARTIAL STRUCTURE & INHARMONICITY: strongly inharmonic — partials deviate substantially from integer multiples, following a free-plate Chladni power law f∝(m+2n)^P (P≈1.7, Rossing & Peterson measured exponent; the audit's corrected plate_modes.h uses exactly this). Mode density grows quadratically with frequency (ρ(f)∝f²), so above ~1-2 kHz the discrete partials merge into a continuous, noise-like band — the characteristic shimmer/sizzle. This is why a hybrid modal+noise body (NoiseBody) is the correct model rather than a sparse modal bank. DECAY TIMES PER BAND: highly band-dependent and time-variant. Low-frequency modes ring long (τ ≈ 3-8 s; edge-strike T60 ≈ 2-4 s for a freely suspended cymbal), while high-frequency modes damp fast (τ < 0.5 s) (Mosico/Arborea measurements; F&R ch.20). Net effect: a struck cymbal is bright at onset then darkens as the HF sizzle dies first — a downward spectral-centroid sweep over the decay, which maps onto decaySkew (tilt energy toward fast/high modes at attack) and a long-tailed low-mode bank. TRANSIENT/CONTACT CLICK: soft for a felt-mallet/bowed orchestral cymbal (little hard tick), more present for a struck closed hit. NOISE CONTENT: dominant. The radiated sound is a dense bright noise field (violet/white tilt) above ~2 kHz, swelling on a roll. PITCH GLIDE: none in the linear regime; cymbals do NOT exhibit a kerthump/tension glide (so PitchEnv Time = 0, tensionModAmt = 0). NONLINEAR BEHAVIOUR: at high drive the cymbal is chaotic — an energy cascade pumps energy from low driven modes up into a dense high-frequency noise spectrum (Touzé & Chaigne; Rossing & Fletcher "Nonlinear vibrations and chaos in gongs and cymbals"). This louder=brighter/broadband-richer behaviour is exactly what Membrum's NonlinearCoupling (amplitude-driven waveshaping, audit M-3/M-4 redesign) approximates, and what the Material Morph swell on the roll variant emulates (timbre brightening as the roll builds). MATERIAL: bronze/B20 alloy plate → metallic, low intrinsic HF damping (Material near max → brightness 0.7+0.3·material, internal noise cutoff clamp(1500+5000·material) pushed high, bodyDampingB3 = 0 for long-ringing highs).
citations: Fletcher & Rossing, The Physics of Musical Instruments, ch.20 (cymbals/gongs)
Touzé & Chaigne; Rossing & Fletcher, Nonlinear vibrations and chaos in gongs and cymbals
plugins/membrum/AUDIT-signal-path-2026-06-07.md (plate Chladni (m+2n)^1.7, NonlinearCoupling redesign, gain-staging, M-9 pan)

archetype: Tabla Bayan (world) — Membrane+Impulse bass drum with iconic heel-pressure pitch gliss; includes 'Ge' resonant open bass and 'Ka' damped strokes
bodyModel: Membrane (Bessel circular drumhead, 48 modes). The bayan is a single circular membrane (pudi/skin) over a clay/metal pot, weighted off-centre by the syahi (iron-loaded tuning paste). Membrane is the only correct body: it is the only one with the Bessel mode shapes, the air-loading frequency correction (Rossing/Tiwari-Gupta), AND the Membrane-only tensionMod pitch glide — which is the defining feature of this archetype. f0 = 500·0.1^size.
exciter: Impulse (raised-cosine click). The bayan is struck with the flat of the fingers/heel of the hand — a fast, fairly hard contact that launches all membrane modes in phase. Impulse is the canonical broadband strike; a small parallel Click layer supplies the finger-contact tick, and a low Noise layer the skin/air slap. Mallet (softer beater) is the alternate exciter for very rounded 'Ghe' open strokes but Impulse is the better default for the percussive bass thump with defined attack. The damped 'Ka/Ke' variant keeps Impulse but kills tension/decay and brightens the click.

params.rationale: Bayan is struck with flat fingers/heel — a fast hard contact launching all modes in phase; Impulse is the canonical broadband strike.
params.citation: chandrakantha.com tabla psychoacoustics; UBC Phys341 Tabla

params.rationale: Bayan is a single circular skin; only Membrane carries Bessel modes + air-loading + the Membrane-only tension glide that defines this drum.
params.citation: Tiwari & Gupta 2017 air-loading; Wikipedia/ Fletcher & Rossing circular membrane

params.physicalTarget: woody/skin (brightness 0.30; baseDecay≈lerp(0.15,0.8,0.30)=0.345 s ×(1+0.1·size)≈0.37 s); low b3 derivation
params.rationale: Goatskin head over a pot is a warm, woody, low-brightness body, not metallic; keeps energy in the low fundamental.
params.citation: chandrakantha psychoacoustics (bayan = few overtones, easy spectrum)

params.rationale: Measured bayan free-strike F0 is 80–100 Hz; size 0.72 lands the head fundamental at ~95 Hz, dead-centre of the measured range.
params.citation: Ashok & Tiwari 2025 (F0∈80–100 Hz); UBC Phys341 (ghe ~100 Hz)

params.physicalTarget: multiplier exp(lerp(ln0.3,ln3.0,0.78))≈1.83× on baseDecay → resonant ring ~0.6–0.7 s (Ge open bass)
params.rationale: The resonant open 'Ge/Ghe' stroke sustains — a single strong ~100 Hz harmonic persists clearly after onset; long decay gives the open ring.
params.citation: arXiv:1510.04880 / researchgate Harmonic & Timbre Analysis (resonant bass = sustained harmonic near 100 Hz)

params.rationale: Open bass strokes contact between the maidan and the syahi, roughly mid-radius — center-ish to favour the low axisymmetric modes, not the rim.
params.citation: chandrakantha psychoacoustics (heel position from edge of maidan/syahi to centre controls tone)

params.rationale: Bass voice sits prominent but below the per-voice rail; leaves master headroom for the N-voice sum.
params.citation: AUDIT gain-staging appendix (−6 dBFS strike budget + bus limiter)

params.rationale: If any tone shaping engages it should darken, matching the dark bass; mostly transparent here (cutoff at max).
No post-body filtering wanted on a clean modal bass; the body itself is already dark.
Filter is bypassed; neutral default.
No filter sweep on the bass; bypass-preserving neutral.
Bayan bass is a clean near-sinusoidal low harmonic; saturation would muddy it. Drive is flavour, not needed here.
No west-coast harmonics in an acoustic drum.
The brief attack 'thump' starts above the settled pitch (membrane stiffens momentarily on the hard strike) before relaxing to the body fundamental — a fast 180→70 Hz drop.
params.citation: Ashok & Tiwari 2025 (bayan pitch tracks tension); 808-style thump precedent

params.rationale: Settles to a low ~70 Hz fundamental, below the ~95 Hz body for an extra deep thump floor on the slack-head bass.
params.citation: Ashok & Tiwari 2025 (F0 80–100 Hz, lower when slack)

params.rationale: Long-ish drop (100 ms) so the pitch-env thump is a slow audible descent, distinct from the separate energy-driven tension gliss.
Linear descent reads as a natural slide rather than an 808 snap.
The syahi LOADS the membrane to make it near-HARMONIC (ratios ~1:2:3); leave the bank at its physical/neutral ratios — do not add inharmonic stretch.

params.physicalTarget: bipolar +0.30: tilts spectral decay toward the LOW modes (highs die faster), global skewBias 1−0.3·0.30=0.91
params.rationale: Open bass loses its (already weak) highs quickly and rings on the fundamental — high modes should decay faster than the low body.
params.citation: researchgate (decay-rate of spectral components discriminates the Ghe stroke; bass = low harmonic persists)

params.rationale: The loaded membrane already supplies its harmonic series; injecting a synthetic 1/k series would dull/clutter it.
Open resonant bass is fairly linear; keep clean. (A small amount can be dialled for hard 'Dha' accents.)
No intra-note timbre sweep needed; the tension glide carries the motion.
Bass strokes are not mutually muting; Ka is a separate pad, not a hat-style choke pair.
Standard routing for the lead bass voice.

params.physicalTarget: 0.60 participation in the global sympathetic engine
params.rationale: The bayan couples to the dayan/resonator in a tabla pair; raise its cross-pad participation so kit-level tomResonance/coupling adds body. (Complexity macro also nudges this.)
Per-param values already set explicitly; keep macro neutral so it doesn't shift them.
Explicit filter/modeInject already set; neutral macro preserves them.
Size set explicitly to 0.72; macro neutral.
Pitch-env/contact set explicitly; neutral macro avoids double-applying.
Adds a touch of sympathetic coupling + dynamic brightening to make the bass feel alive in the pair context, matching the existing tabla kit voicing.

params.physicalTarget: low parallel filtered-noise layer (≈−18 dBFS region)
params.rationale: A small skin/air slap component on contact; the bayan bass is mostly tonal so noise stays low.
params.citation: researchgate (resonant bass = harmonic, little noise; damped strokes = diffuse spectrum)

params.rationale: Dark skin-noise to match the low body; keeps the slap from sounding hissy.
Mild emphasis, no pitched beep on the lowpass path.
Short skin-slap that dies before the tonal ring, just a contact texture.
Pink noise = natural broadband skin/air slap, not bright sizzle.

params.physicalTarget: contact tick mixed (≈−18 dBFS, also half-amplitude into the exciter)
params.rationale: Finger/heel contact gives a soft attack definition on the open bass; moderate so the tonal body dominates.
params.citation: researchgate (onset transient present on bass strokes)

params.rationale: Short, soft finger contact — a thud-tick, not a sharp stick crack.
Dull-ish contact thud matching a hand strike on a bass head, not a bright rim tick.

params.physicalTarget: 0.2+0.30·49.8 = 15.1 s⁻¹ flat damping floor (overrides Decay-derived b1)
params.rationale: Low flat damping = long open-bass RT60; the resonant 'Ge' sustains its ~95 Hz fundamental.
Very low f² damping keeps what little high content rings naturally; the loaded membrane is near-harmonic so highs aren't crushed, but stay quiet (woody material).

params.physicalTarget: 0.62 toward the Rossing air-loaded mode curve (lowers the lowest Bessel modes)
params.rationale: A real large bass membrane over an enclosed pot is strongly air-loaded — the low modes are depressed (deeper, less whistly), exactly the Tiwari-Gupta measured effect for an Indian drum.
params.citation: Tiwari & Gupta 2017 'Effects of air loading on the acoustics of an Indian musical drum'; Rossing 1982

params.rationale: Small organic detune — real heads have slight asymmetry/frequency splitting near the 2nd harmonic (membrane-tension asymmetry); a little scatter mimics that without destroying the harmonic feel.
params.citation: Ashok & Tiwari 2025 (asymmetry → frequency splitting near 2nd harmonic, degenerate modes near 3rd)

params.physicalTarget: 0.32 feedforward head→shell drive (engaged, since Secondary on)
params.rationale: The pot/shell of the bayan adds a coupled body resonance under the head; moderate coupling adds bass weight.
params.citation: Bayan = membrane over clay/metal pot (Britannica Tabla; UBC)

params.physicalTarget: on: 24-mode secondary shell bank spun up
params.rationale: Models the bayan's resonating pot/shell beneath the head.
The pot resonance sits below the head fundamental, reinforcing the sub.
A short, fairly damped metal/clay-pot ring — adds weight on the attack then gets out of the way of the head's sustain.

params.physicalTarget: depth 0.78·3.3e-5 energy scale → energy-driven upward gliss, ~1.5–2 semitones at hard-strike modal-energy peak, clamped ≤+3 st, relaxing as the body decays
params.rationale: THE defining bayan feature: heel/wrist pressure bends the pitch (Zakir Hussain melodies). Highest tensionMod in the library — a hard 'Ge' kicks the pitch up then relaxes. This is Membrane-only and exactly the energy-dependent tension model.
params.citation: Ashok & Tiwari 2025; chandrakantha (heel pressure raises pitch; push-then-release gliss); Avanzini-Marogna-Bank 2012 JASA (ΔT ∝ energy)

params.rationale: Lead bass voice of the kit.
A simple one-segment thump is enough; the tension gliss provides the expressive bend, not a scripted 3-point env.
No-op with knee off; neutral.
No-op with knee off.
No-op with knee off.
The bayan is the LEFT-hand drum of the pair; nudge it slightly left for the natural stereo image of a seated player. (Dayan would sit slightly right.)

defaultedParams.param: PitchEnv Knee / Mid / Mid Frac / Curve2
